news_summary: Western Metallica announced the start of a first drilling program in October at its Luz Maria copper (Cu-Mo) porphyry prospect within the Caña Brava project in Peru, following permits. The news is an exploration sentiment driver with no immediate supply or demand change; nickel and aluminium are not addressed. | GGL Resources announced Teck’s option agreement for GGL’s Le Champ copper-molybdenum-gold porphyry target in Nevada, including cash option payments and a production-decision milestone. The release supports potential future copper/molybdenum development but provides no near-term output, so any immediate copper price impact is likely limited.

Carnaby Resources reported results from a VTEM magnetic survey at the Greater Duchess copper-gold JV, identifying multiple new undrilled conductive targets and highlighting the Mohawk corridor with prior 21m at 2% copper. Management expects immediate drilling focus and possible resource expansion, but no near-term output change is provided. | Next Week At A Glance highlights broad material-sector support from PBOC stimulus, but the only direct industrial-metals link is Nickel Industries buying a 51% stake in Indonesia’s Siduarsi nickel-cobalt project. This can marginally lift near-term nickel supply expectations, supporting prices modestly. | Villagers in Indonesia’s Sulawesi protest nickel exploration and HPAL smelting operations, citing land loss, environmental and safety concerns, and potential livelihood disruption. The story links Indonesia’s expanding nickel supply—including HPAL smelters—to persistent oversupply and weak price outlooks, implying bearish near-term sentiment for nickel. | The article investigates Indonesia’s nickel industry in Sulawesi, visiting nickel mines, smelters and local communities. It describes environmental pollution from mining and coal-powered smelting, unsafe working conditions, and alleged illegal land seizures. The narrative links the nickel boom’s social and environmental risks to potential challenges for nickel production and costs.

Power Nickel announced assay results from the first five holes of its summer 2024 drilling at the polymetallic Lion Zone discovery. Reported intervals range from about 5–12 metres with copper-equivalent grades between 2.36% and 5.93%, which may strengthen expectations for future nickel and copper resource development. | Power Nickel reported assay results from the Lion Zone discovery, including multiple copper- and nickel-bearing intercepts (e.g., several meters of %Cu and sub-% Ni, plus CuEq). While positive drill results can support sentiment for future nickel and copper supply, the impact is likely small given the exploration stage. | Tsingshan cut Indonesia ferronickel (nickel pig iron) output due to persistent nickel ore shortages linked to delays in mining quota approvals. Nickel production costs rose and some furnaces reportedly switched to other output. Philippine ore prices jumped sharply, while Chinese NPI prices rose only modestly, implying bearish near-term nickel sentiment. | QC Copper announced an all-share acquisition of Cuprum, adding the Thierry Copper Project and increasing its copper resource base. The Thierry asset historically produced copper and contains minor nickel, with references to past concentrate shipments. The deal is positioned for future development, not immediate production or supply changes. | Orecap announced QC Copper’s offer to acquire Cuprum in an all-share deal, adding the Thierry copper project and increasing QC Copper’s copper resource base. The transaction, expected to close by end-December 2024, strengthens Canada’s copper developer footprint. Thierry also historically produced concentrate with minor nickel content.

Nearctic Nickel Mines Inc. announced it has divested all remaining interest in Ungava Mines Inc., ending its indirect interest in UMEI. UMEI is a plaintiff in an ongoing action against the Quebec government alleging disguised expropriation. The update mainly changes company exposure, not disclosed nickel output or timing. | BHP said it will temporarily suspend Nickel West operations due to global nickel oversupply and high costs, which has pressured nickel prices. The piece argues the event could benefit Nico Resources’ Wingellina project via lower competitive pressure and investor attention, but impact on near-term nickel tightness is unclear. | Canada Nickel says it has started submitting the federal Impact Statement for its Crawford nickel sulphide project and expects to complete it within six weeks, targeting permits in 2025. The company frames the process as advancing permitting progress. This can modestly support near-term nickel market sentiment via improved supply outlook. | Prairie Operating reportedly gained additional production and increased acreage via a Nickel Road M&A transaction. The update points to improved nickel resource access and/or output potential. In the near term, incremental supply expectations can weigh on nickel pricing, with no clear information here affecting copper or aluminium.

Power Nickel released new drill assay results from the Lion Zone at its Nisk property, highlighting longer and higher-grade intersections with notable nickel and copper grades. The company says the discovery appears substantially bigger than previously envisioned, improving confidence in mineral continuity and supporting expectations for future Ni-Cu supply. | Power Nickel reported assay results from three additional drill holes at its Lion Zone polymetallic discovery. Hole 70 returned 32m of 0.18% Ni plus 3.62% Cu and other metals, alongside additional Au and Ag. The update strengthens the perceived resource potential and is mildly supportive for nickel and copper sentiment. | Grid Metals outlines 4 km of copper-dominant mineralization with associated nickel at its MM copper/nickel project’s Eagle gabbro target in Manitoba. The company plans inaugural drilling and additional geophysical surveys. It provides grab-sample grades but no new resource estimate or production impacts. | Grid Metals reported surface copper/nickel mineralization along a ~4 km Eagle gabbro corridor at its MM project in Manitoba, with grades up to 1.3% Cu and associated nickel/cobalt/PGM values. The company plans geophysical work and inaugural drilling this fall after permits, but no production or supply changes were announced. | Granite Creek Copper reports discovery of a new “Gap Zone” at its Carmacks copper-gold-silver project in Yukon, with copper mineralization in 3 of 4 holes and results pending from sampled sections. The company suggests potential to expand resources and mine life, but this is exploration news without immediate supply impact.

Lifezone Metals signed an MOU with JOGMEC to supply nickel from the Kabanga Nickel Project in Tanzania to Japanese battery demand. The arrangement supports JOGMEC’s acquisition of cleaner metals and references hydromet refining at Kahama. It signals incremental nickel supply visibility, with a likely supportive tone for nickel prices.

Elliott Associates lost its appeal against dismissal of its lawsuit against the LME over billions of dollars of cancelled nickel trades. The LME annulled $12 billion in nickel trades in March 2022 after a record price spike. The Court of Appeal upheld that the LME acted lawfully in cancelling to prevent a “death spiral.” | Elliott Investment Management’s UK appeal was rejected after the LME halted a runaway short squeeze in the nickel market. The decision follows the exchange’s controversial move to stop squeeze-driven volatility. Such regulatory intervention can cool speculative pressure and weigh on nickel prices near term. | BNN Bloomberg reports that Elliott has lost a UK appeal over the LME’s nickel market shutdown. The decision highlights ongoing issues around trading or settlement of nickel on the exchange, which can worsen near-term supply/availability expectations and pressure nickel prices over coming sessions. | A Court of Appeal upheld the LME’s decision to suspend and cancel nickel trades in March 2022 after a dramatic price spike. The court said the LME acted lawfully to prevent a potential “death spiral.” The article notes nickel’s importance to stainless and batteries, but the judgment is retrospective.

EV Nickel reported maiden drilling results at the CarLang trend, intersecting near-surface nickel mineralization in new zones (B, C and E) over more than 7 km of strike. CarLang C delivered the highest grades to date, including up to 0.96% Ni over short intervals, extending the potential resource footprint. | First Atlantic Nickel Corp. began core drilling at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland after summer sampling expanded visible awaruite (a naturally occurring nickel-iron alloy) targets along a 30 km trend. The company aims to test new, widely spaced nickel targets, with results pending, implying limited immediate market impact. | EV Nickel Inc. announced maiden diamond drilling at the CarLang trend intersected three new zones of near-surface nickel mineralization, including the highest grades to date. Results included intervals up to 0.96% Ni and a 7km strike extension, with mineralization appearing magmatic. The discovery remains open for expansion.

Nickel 28 Capital Corp. announced confirmed cash distributions from its 8.56% interest in the Ramu nickel-cobalt project, including about US$4.0 million for H1 2024 and repayment of part of its construction debt. Management attributes distributions to efficient operations despite low nickel and cobalt prices, with timing pending confirmation. | The interview says Indonesia’s nickel industry is scaling into an EV battery-material ecosystem, citing commissioned precursor and lithium refining capacity and existing copper-foil operations near Freeport. It also notes forced-labor/environmental allegations and IRA rules limiting subsidized EV inputs from highly foreign-controlled entities, plus LFP’s nickel-free shift. | Canada Nickel received a C$4.38 million conditional contribution from Canada’s Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund to fund electrical infrastructure pre-implementation studies for its Crawford nickel sulphide project. The studies run April 2024 to June 2025, supporting project development and eventual nickel production, but it’s early-stage financing.

Core Nickel Corp. announced a non-brokered private placement of up to $252,000 to fund general working capital, including potential flow-through unit proceeds. As a nickel explorer, the financing may support future exploration, but it provides no immediate production or supply impact, suggesting limited near-term effect on nickel prices.

BHP is placing its WA Nickel West division on care and maintenance for at least three years, cutting jobs and effectively closing parts of Australia’s nickel supply chain. The report links the decision to global nickel oversupply and falling prices, alongside other mine closures including First Quantum’s Ravensthorpe. | BHP has shut down production at its Kalgoorlie Nickel Smelter, placing it on care and maintenance after decades of continuous operation. The closure follows broader shutdowns in Western Australia’s nickel mines, with nickel export values forecast to fall sharply. Job losses underscore tightening local supply prospects for nickel. | Suvo Strategic Minerals reports lab trials showing nickel slag can be converted into high-strength, low-carbon cement. The nickel slag is supplied by Indonesia producer PT Huadi Nickel-Alloy Indonesia, which has large stockpiles. Further trials aim to qualify additional cement properties, supporting potential offtake arrangements.

Eramet cut its 2024 targets for Comilog and PT Weda Bay Nickel amid weaker carbon steel demand in China and fewer nickel ore sales permits from Indonesia than expected. Nickel ore external volume for PT WBN was revised down to 29 million wet tons, indicating reduced supply expectations. | Vale reports a six-year high in iron ore output, and the article states that its copper and nickel production also increased. Stronger production trends can improve near-term supply expectations, which generally supports prices for copper and nickel over the following days to weeks. | Vale reported six-year-high iron ore output, and the title also notes that copper and nickel production climbed. If investors expect improved near-term supply from Vale, it can pressure copper and nickel prices over the following days as market balances adjust.

Grid Metals reports receiving permits and commencing a maiden drill program at the Eagle gabbro within its MM copper/nickel project in Manitoba, with geophysical surveys running in tandem. Assays are expected by mid-November/early next month. The update is exploratory and does not change near-term supply. | Grid Metals reports the initiation of its maiden drill program at the Eagle gabbro within its MM copper/nickel project in Manitoba, supported by permits and geophysical surveys. Management expects initial assays by mid-November and cites potential copper-dominant mineralization. The update is exploratory and not directly tied to near-term production changes. | Michigan DNR acquired 8,392 acres of mineral rights in Porcupine Mountains Wilderness State Park in a mineral-for-mineral land exchange, citing prospective copper and nickel resources. The article stresses the transaction does not impact Highland Copper’s Copperwood project, which would access minerals outside the park with no surface disturbance. | Global Ferronickel Holdings (FNI) is in talks with Asian and European firms to build a nickel processing facility, adding to existing plants in Palawan and Surigao run by Nickel Asia. The government is also seeking incentives and clearer rules for critical-mineral processing, which supports nickel supply outlook sentiment.

Indonesia’s Energy and Mineral Resources Minister Bahlil Lahadalia said the nickel downstream industry relies on foreign workers to speed progress. He cited faster smelter construction by Chinese workers and described efforts to repatriate foreign labor when local skills are available, though transitions cannot happen instantly. | Indonesia’s mining minister said the government will manage nickel ore supply and demand to support prices, using smelter prices and demand as parameters for market balance. He cited quota-approval delays that forced some smelters to import ores from the Philippines, highlighting near-term supply-control efforts. | SMM reports Indonesia’s Ministry of Mines adjusted PT WBN’s laterite nickel ore sales quota (2024-2026), raising annual sales volume to 32 mln wmt. Markets reacted with bearish expectations for Indonesian nickel ore premiums, though current prices stay stable due to already-signed orders. Indonesia may soften premiums later this month; Philippines remains firmer.

SMM data show China’s September nickel ore imports fell 7.4% MoM and 23.5% YoY, with declines across both sulfide and oxide. Philippine ore remains the main source, but Indonesia’s RKAB quota progress expected in October may reduce Philippine demand and shift exports toward China, with possible typhoon-driven shipping delays.

SMM reports nickel prices have fallen sharply, with SHFE dropping below 126,000 yuan/mt and LME trading around $16,300–$16,500. The main drivers are improved Indonesian nickel-ore supply expectations via RKAB approvals and broader macro risk aversion ahead of the US election. High-grade NPI remains firmer but may retreat on weakening stainless fundamentals.

Anglo American said Q3 copper output fell 13% year on year because of a planned closure of a processing plant at its Los Bronces mine in Chile. It also flagged a potential coal-division sale. Nickel and platinum guidance was raised, but the reported copper production decline is the main near-term pressure. | Anglo American said copper production fell 13% quarter-on-quarter due to the planned closure of Chile’s Los Bronces plant, partially offset by higher grades at El Soldado and improvements at Quellaveco expected later. Nickel output rose 6%, though Codemin refinery issues partially offset gains. | Anglo American said Q3 copper production fell to 181Kt versus 209Kt, citing planned closure of Los Bronces, partially offset by higher grades at El Soldado. Nickel production rose and full-year nickel guidance was raised to 38-39Kt. Annual copper outlook remains within 730-790Kt, tempering market impact. | Anglo American said Q3 copper output fell to 181Kt from 209Kt due to the planned closure of the smaller, costlier Los Bronces plant, partially offset by higher grades at El Soldado. Nickel output rose to 9.9kt and full-year nickel guidance was raised, while copper guidance stayed broadly within 730–790Kt. | Core Nickel Corp. announced changes to its board, welcoming Paul Reid and Marc Pais, and noting upcoming early-2025 drilling/testing plans on its Thompson Nickel Belt nickel projects near existing mills. The article is primarily corporate governance with no immediate production, supply, or inventory impact for nickel prices. | Talon Metals announces its maiden drill hole at the Boulderdash target in Michigan intersected 99.92 meters of copper-nickel mineralization, including intervals with stated copper and nickel equivalents. The company plans additional drilling and EM surveys. The discovery is early-stage, near existing nickel operations, but not production. | Talon Metals announced its first drill hole at the Boulderdash target in Michigan intersected 99.92 meters of copper-nickel mineralization, including high-grade CuEq/NiEq intervals, and plans additional drilling and EM surveys. The release frames it as a potential domestic critical minerals resource near an existing U.S. nickel mine, but provides no production. | Indonesia’s new government targets higher economic growth and plans downstream nickel capacity expansion via hydrometallurgy, which could support nickel demand and output. At the same time, the government aims to raise fiscal revenue and may adjust taxes (higher VAT, potential CIT reduction), creating mixed effects on mining and processing costs, so near-term price impact is uncertain. | NewRange Copper Nickel held an open house to build momentum for plans to develop two copper-nickel deposits near Hoyt Lakes. The project follows Glencore/Teck’s earlier PolyMet efforts that faced permit reversals and lawsuits. NewRange seeks to reapply for key permits in 2025, with mining not expected before 2030. | NewRange Copper Nickel held an open house to build momentum for two copper-nickel deposits near Hoyt Lakes, with plans to salvage and refurbish an old concentrator. The project faces permitting and legal setbacks, including a revoked Clean Water Act permit, and operations are not expected before 2030, making near-term price effects limited. | Fastmarkets says Chinese nickel pig iron (NPI) prices climbed 20-30 yuan to 1,030-1,050 yuan per nickel unit, the highest since Nov 10, 2023. A major stainless steel tender lifted NPI offers on rumors of joint feedstock price hikes, citing Indonesia ore supply shortages and higher production costs.

Nickel premiums were flat globally for full-plate cathodes, 4×4 cathodes, and briquettes, even as the LME three-month nickel contract hit its lowest 5pm close since mid-September. LME warehouse stocks in Rotterdam are ample and import arbitrage losses narrowed slightly. | Flying Nickel announced a final court order for an arrangement with Norway House Cree Nation to acquire the company’s Minago nickel project in Manitoba for $8 million cash and related share surrender and royalties. Closing is expected by end of October, subject to customary approvals. No operational or supply changes are detailed.

Stillwater Critical Minerals signed a letter of intent for Granite Creek Copper to acquire a 90% interest in the Duke Island Cu-Ni-PGE project in Alaska. The announcement is mainly a project ownership and advancement plan, with no near-term production impact specified, limiting immediate effects on copper and nickel prices.

The piece argues that Indonesia’s nickel downstreaming push—despite rapid smelter growth and cheap feedstock—may not translate into significant battery capacity or higher nickel value. It notes most nickel output still goes to stainless steel and that foreign competitors are bypassing steps. Beijing’s advice to keep EV components in China raises stakes. | Perseverance Metals reported new high-grade nickel discoveries at its Lac Gayot project (up to ~1.54% Ni in surface samples) and highlighted an airborne HeliTEM2 survey over the property. The company continues nickel-copper-PGE exploration, but provides no production or confirmed near-term supply changes. | Perseverance Metals reported new high-grade nickel discoveries at its Lac Gayot nickel-copper-PGE project in Québec, including up to 1.54% nickel in surface samples, plus an airborne HeliTEM survey. The company also identified spodumene-bearing pegmatites, but the direct market impact is mainly nickel-copper exploration sentiment. | Sterling Metals reported high-grade copper and molybdenum values from surface grab samples at its Copper Road porphyry target in Ontario, with an ongoing 3D IP survey to guide drilling. The article is copper-related but contains no production or reserve/timing updates, limiting any near-term price impact. | Sterling Metals reported initial Phase 1 exploration results at its Copper Road project in Ontario, with surface grab samples returning up to 15.9% Cu and 4.84% Mo, plus evidence of a ~2 km footprint of porphyry mineralization. The news is development-focused and unlikely to materially move near-term copper prices, especially beyond sentiment.

Saudi Arabia’s Ma’aden/Manara says it is in advanced talks over acquiring a minority stake in a Zambian copper mine, expecting a deal by year-end. Prior Reuters sources linked the stake purchase to First Quantum Minerals’ Zambian copper and nickel assets, but no immediate operational changes are mentioned. | Pan Global initiated an IP geophysical survey at the Bravo copper target within Spain’s Escacena Project to refine drilling locations. Gravity, soil geochemistry, and helicopter EM anomalies suggest concealed VMS sulphide mineralization possibly hosting copper. Financing is on track, but no drilling results or output changes are announced. | BeMetals announced 2024 shallow aircore drilling at Zambia’s Pangeni Copper Project, extending the Nkala Zone footprint and identifying several new copper anomalies, with further core results expected in coming weeks. The news is exploration-focused and does not indicate immediate production or market supply changes for copper. | Glencore reported lower nickel production in the first three quarters of 2024, driven by Koniambo’s move to care and maintenance. Higher output from Australia’s Murrin Murrin partly offset the decline, and with IRA-compliant briquettes becoming more constrained, the nickel briquette premium in the US rose sharply. | Reuters reports companies are restarting shuttered copper mines and other abandoned assets to fast-track additional supply for the clean-energy and AI demand outlook. Examples include Canada, Quebec and Spain reopenings, with output targets over the next years. The revival is largely driven by recent high copper (and nickel) prices. | Talon Metals announced discovery of 99.92 meters of copper and nickel mineralization at the Boulderdash target in Michigan’s Upper Peninsula. Using surface prospecting, geophysics, and maiden drilling, the company says results confirm the source of mineralized boulders. It highlights potential domestic critical mineral supply. | Flying Nickel Mining Corp. announced the closing of an $8 million sale of its Minago Nickel Project to Norway House Cree Nation via a purchaser entity. The transaction transfers the project and related obligations and cancels NHCN’s shareholding. It signals asset repositioning but provides no immediate production or supply figures affecting nickel prices.

MIT researchers assess nickel’s growing role in EVs and renewables and the risks in its supply chain, emphasizing Indonesia’s dominance and coal-based processing’s high emissions, plus land, pollution, and social impacts. Policymakers are urged to enforce sustainability standards, but no immediate market action is announced.

SolGold began geotechnical investigations at its Cascabel copper-gold porphyry project in Ecuador, including drilling and seismic assessments to refine ground conditions for mine infrastructure. The pre-feasibility plan targets 3.2Mt copper (0.60%) and schedules further feasibility work. The step is incremental and not a near-term copper supply shock. | Copper and other metals reportedly gained as the U.S. dollar weakened and markets built hopes for China stimulus. The combination tends to improve risk sentiment and expected industrial demand, generally supporting prices for industrial metals like copper, aluminium, and nickel over the coming days. | Sumitomo is resuming operations at its Ambatovy nickel project in Madagascar after production was paused by a pipeline rupture. The mine has missed prior output targets and faced write-downs, and a nickel export revenue decline is noted. With the market already weak, restored supply expectations may support nickel prices.

L&F says it will start producing NCM cathodes with 95% nickel content for BEV lithium-ion batteries by end-2024, with shipments to cell manufacturers beginning in December. The company cites technology progress from N90 to N95 and expects more volume, supporting incremental nickel demand sentiment. | Inomin and Sumitomo have signed a non-binding term sheet to jointly develop the Beaver-Lynx nickel project in British Columbia. Sumitomo could earn up to 80% via C$8m in exploration spend in two phases. Early results suggest significant nickel volumes, though the JV remains subject to due diligence. | South Korean materials maker L&F plans to mass produce NCM battery cathodes with 95% nickel content starting in December and supply Korean battery makers and US EV manufacturers. The move suggests higher near-term consumption of nickel-rich cathode materials, which can lift short-term nickel demand expectations and support nickel prices. | Canada Nickel announced it intersected 3.9 metres of massive sulphide within 12.7 metres of mineralization at its Bannockburn Property near Timmins, Ontario. The company said this is the first massive sulphide drilled in the Timmins Nickel District, suggesting improved prospects for nickel mineralization at the site.

Poseidon Nickel said its Windarra tailings agreement with Encore Minerals is now unconditional after meeting conditions. The project—about 720 km from Perth—includes processing gold tailings using glycine leaching technology and could begin production, receiving upfront payments and royalties. Better development prospects likely support nickel sentiment near term.

The piece notes Trump’s US election win could bring tariff hikes on China, pressuring base metals in the short term. Offsetting that, brokers see an accelerating copper deficit from electrification demand and expect aluminium supply deficits. Nickel is expected to remain weak with rising LME inventories and softer demand. | The piece links Donald Trump’s US election win to potential tariff hikes on China, a short-term headwind for base metals. It also highlights RBC’s view of accelerating copper deficits from electrification and Wilsons’ forecast of an aluminium swing to deficit as China’s supply growth is constrained. Nickel remains pressured by inventories and soft demand.

Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt is seeking about $2.7bn financing for a Ford-backed HPAL nickel processing facility in Indonesia, using ore from Vale’s Pomalaa Block mine to produce battery-grade nickel. The fundraising comes while nickel prices are subdued near a four-year low due to weaker stainless and slower EV demand growth.

Indonesia’s PT Vale Indonesia and GEM signed a collaboration for a US$1.4bn HPAL project in Central Sulawesi aimed at a net-zero processing site for nickel. The project targets at least 60,000 tonnes of contained nickel in MHP annually, alongside R&D, ESG initiatives, and community development. | Canada Nickel announced assay results from drillhole BAN24-18 at its Bannockburn project, including 3.95% nickel over 4 metres and up to 4.65% nickel over 1 metre. The interval also reports minor copper (0.40% over 12 metres). The update supports bullish near-term sentiment for nickel.

Premium Nickel Resources extended the study phase for its Selebi Mines project by one year to complete an economic study and a US$25 million milestone payment. It continues drilling and metallurgical/engineering work, and reports historic drill core data including nickel and copper to support an upcoming resource estimate. | GEM and PT Vale Indonesia agreed on a $1.4bn nickel HPAL facility in Sulawesi, aimed at producing at least 60,000t/year of contained nickel in MHP for battery use. The project includes R&D and ESG components and may expand into anode/precursor production downstream.

Cumberland Resources Nickel Corp. announced a non-brokered private placement of units priced at $0.03, raising up to $750,000. Net proceeds will be used for property development and working capital related to its St. Anthony nickel property in Newfoundland. The financing could support near-term project plans, slightly improving nickel sentiment. | Indonesia’s hotel association raised concerns about a nickel project on Gag Island in Raja Ampat, arguing mining could harm the marine ecosystem and tourism. Antam’s unit bought 30% of a nickel smelter for $102 million, potentially accelerating mining. The piece highlights environmental risks more than immediate supply impacts. | QC Copper completed a cost-effective resampling program on historical drill core at the Thierry Copper Project (K1 Zone) to better delineate disseminated chalcopyrite/pyrite mineralization and potentially expand resources. Cuprum owns the project; a related business combination is expected to close in December. No immediate production or supply impacts are described. | Markets are watching for the incoming US administration to tighten IRA Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) rules, likely limiting Chinese-linked nickel from qualifying for EV tax credits. Allegations of nickel misreported as IRA-compliant are also driving scrutiny. Analysts expect automakers and battery makers to rush to secure non-FEOC nickel units, tightening supply expectations. | Nickel prices have slid toward a four-year low as weaker-than-expected electric vehicle demand and China’s real estate downturn weigh on expectations for battery-metal demand. The move highlights how softer end-demand in EVs and construction can quickly pressure nickel pricing, erasing prior gains tied to the EV boom.

Class 1 Nickel filed an NI 43-101 Technical Report supporting an updated Mineral Resource Estimate for the Dundonald South nickel sulphide deposit, including higher indicated nickel tonnage and continued drilling plans. While it does not change production, the update is supportive for near-term nickel sentiment and expectations.

New Caledonian nickel producer Prony Resources will restart operations from Monday after a six-month shutdown linked to riots. The restart follows restored power, water and road access, though no production timeline is provided. Wider unrest has disrupted other local producers, so supply recovery could support nickel prices. | High-nickel pig iron prices are plunging further, with SMM 8-12% NPI averaging 1,005.2 yuan per nickel point and the Indonesian NPI FOB index also falling. Weak stainless demand/off-season and sluggish procurement coincide with macro pressure (Fed/LEM), pushing both NPI and pure nickel downward and widening the NPI/electrolytic nickel discount. | Indonesia’s nickel ore imports climbed to 9.3 million tons in January–October 2024, versus 161,917 tons a year earlier. Reuters links the surge to record purchases from the Philippines since April as smelter demand increased, while Indonesia delayed mining quotas amid heavy rains disrupting mining activity. | SMM reports Indonesian laterite nickel ore premiums are flat and prices stable amid uncertainty over RKAB quota consumption by year-end. In the Philippines, limited northern shipments and firmer FOB/CIF mid-grade nickel ore pricing are gradually strengthening prices, though weakening domestic high-grade NPI tempers restocking. Overall nickel ore sentiment is supportive short term.

The article discusses nickel price momentum and volatility, citing oversupply from Indonesia and mine curtailments elsewhere, alongside weaker Chinese economic/demand conditions. It notes Chinese stimulus briefly lifted nickel prices in September, but prices retreated after the initial surge, implying near-term bearish pressure. | The piece highlights nickel’s volatile first half and a Q3 oversupply situation driven by increased laterite supply from Indonesia, prompting curtailments in other regions. It cites weak Chinese demand despite stimulus-induced price support in September, noting prices retreated again. It frames Carpenter Technology as a nickel alloys producer. | The piece reviews nickel market conditions, citing oversupply of nickel from Asian/Indonesian sources and weak Chinese demand due to a strained real estate sector and deflation. It notes brief price support from Chinese stimulus in September, but prices later retreated, keeping nickel pressure negative. | The article reviews nickel market conditions affecting “best nickel stocks,” noting strong H1 momentum but Q3 2024 volatility. It cites laterite nickel supply growth from Indonesia/Asia driving oversupply, while weak Chinese demand from deflation and real estate pressures offset battery demand growth, and September stimulus only temporarily supported prices before retreating. | The piece highlights strong but volatile nickel momentum in H1 and Q3 2024, attributing weakness to oversupply of laterite nickel from Indonesia and mine curtailments in other regions, while Chinese demand remains constrained by a difficult real-estate backdrop. A September stimulus briefly supported prices, but they retreated again. | The article reviews nickel price momentum and volatility, citing oversupply of laterite nickel from Indonesia and weaker Chinese demand from stainless-steel and broader economic deflation. It notes short-lived support from Chinese stimulus in September. It also mentions LEG uses nickel-based alloys but provides no direct nickel supply/demand catalyst. | The piece reviews nickel market conditions and ATI’s positioning. It highlights H1 strength but Q3 volatility tied to oversupply from Indonesia, mine curtailments elsewhere, and weak Chinese demand. Even after Chinese stimulus briefly supported nickel prices in September, prices retreated, implying ongoing downside risk for nickel. | The article discusses nickel’s H1 momentum but highlights Q3 2024 volatility driven by Indonesian supply growth, mine curtailments elsewhere, and persistent oversupply. It notes China demand softness from macro issues despite stimulus measures that briefly lifted nickel prices before prices retreated again. | The article reviews nickel price momentum and volatility in 2024, citing Indonesian laterite supply, nickel oversupply, and China stimulus-induced swings in demand. It also highlights Vale’s involvement in nickel and copper and an agreement for a $1.42B Indonesian nickel plant, but provides no clear short-term market disruption.

The piece says a Trump-era, more protectionist trade environment could make an Indonesia–US free-trade agreement unlikely. It also notes the IRA favors EV supply chains in US/FTA countries, affecting Indonesian nickel shipments that are widely sent to China, while Tesla may gradually reduce nickel dependence. | Indonesia’s Energy and Mineral Resources Ministry defended local smelters’ imports of nickel ore despite abundant domestic resources. It said the imports follow individual company strategy and help extend domestic nickel supply as reserves deplete. Indonesia also remains a major producer but imported much nickel ore from the Philippines in 2023 and 2024. | Prony Resources plans to restart operations at the Goro mine and battery-grade nickel plant in New Caledonia after a six-month suspension from local riots. The workforce return revives a major regional nickel source (with battery-grade nickel and cobalt output), potentially lifting short-term supply expectations and weighing on nickel prices. | Talon Metals’ drilling at the Boulderdash target in Michigan intersected substantial copper-nickel sulphide intervals across several drill holes, including up to 110.3 meters of mineralization with up to 40% sulphides, and indicators of possible massive sulphides. Assays are pending; results could modestly support copper and nickel sentiment. | Vital Battery Metals reported assay results from its Sting Copper Project, including a grab sample with 9.3% Cu and soil sampling showing a 300+ meter Cu–Zn–As anomaly. The update supports exploration potential but does not indicate near-term supply changes, limiting likely immediate effects on copper prices and none on nickel or aluminium.

SMM reports Indonesia is considering changes to the non-tax state revenue (PNBP) interpretation and resource fee structure for laterite nickel ore. A proposed 2% royalty for ore below 1.5% grade is aimed at supporting battery materials. It also updates RKAB approvals and quota volumes for 2024-2026. | Xanadu Mines announced four new high-grade copper targets at its Sant Tolgoi project in Mongolia, with surface rock-chip assays up to 2.1% Cu and geophysical anomalies pointing to mineralised intrusions. The planned reconnaissance drilling will test for massive sulphide copper and possibly nickel, but no production is yet confirmed. | Electra Battery Materials received a ten-year exploration permit from the US Forest Service covering 91 drill pad locations for its Idaho Cobalt Belt projects, including the Iron Creek copper-cobalt deposit. The permit adds regulatory certainty to resume exploration, but the news is more developmental than an immediate supply change. | Xanadu Mines announced high-grade surface copper assay results from its Sant Tolgoi project in Mongolia, identifying four drill targets based on geochemistry and geophysics, with planned reconnaissance drilling in Q2 2025. Assays show copper up to 2.1% and very low nickel (<0.06%) at surface, implying exploration potential only.

Ivanhoe Mines announced the official reopening of the Kipushi zinc-copper-germanium mine in the DRC. The restart is expected to bring additional copper supply from the existing Kamoa-Kakula/Kipushi complex over time. Ceremonial confirmation supports near-term optimism on copper output and project ramp-up. | Alaska Energy Metals reported assay results from three drill holes at the Canwell claim block, intersecting wide zones of nickel-bearing polymetallic mineralization and providing evidence of sulfide/metallic-hosted nickel. The company plans further drilling, but the update is not production-related, limiting immediate supply impact.

Xanadu Mines announced strong surface copper assay results from its Sant Tolgoi Project in Mongolia and identified four drill targets. The company plans 3,000m of reconnaissance drilling to test for high-grade massive sulphide copper and nickel, with follow-up drilling expected in early 2025.

Aldebaran announced an updated mineral resource estimate for its Altar copper-gold project in Argentina, showing a substantial increase versus 2021, including 22.01 billion pounds of copper. The company also plans the next study work (PEA in Q2 2025), adding near-term development optimism for copper.

Minnesota’s Department of Natural Resources has delayed its decision on a key permit for a proposed copper-nickel mine. The delay may push back timelines for potential future production, improving near-term supply expectations. This is supportive for both copper and nickel prices, though magnitude is uncertain while the project remains pending. | Minnesota DNR delayed its decision on a key permit for a proposed copper-nickel mine. The delay likely postpones potential production timelines, increasing near-term uncertainty around future supply. That typically supports bearish sentiment for copper and nickel as markets recalibrate expectations for when the project could contribute output. | First Atlantic Nickel applied for additional drill permits and plans a direct access route to accelerate winter drilling at the RPM Zone. The discovery hole intersected visible awaruite (nickel-iron alloy) mineralization from surface to depth. Phase 2 drilling is planned pending permits and assay results. | The article is focused on cobalt but repeatedly ties it to copper and nickel. It forecasts copper prices to remain strong, supporting further mined supply growth in the DRC, while noting nickel’s short-term price weakness despite Indonesia’s expanding HPAL-linked output. Net signals for copper vs nickel are mixed.

Prosecutors allege nickel trading businessman Ng Yu Zhi ran a fraud in which investors were promised discounted nickel purchases from Poseidon Nickel Limited and profitable resale. The Envy entities allegedly never bought or sold nickel, instead using new investor funds to pay earlier ones, via forged contracts and documents. | GT Resources announced a Phase I BHEM geophysical survey and reconnaissance work at its North Rock copper-nickel-PGE project in Ontario. The program targets historic holes to find net-textured to massive sulphides beneath shallow drilling, with a historic copper grade noted, but no near-term production impact. | GT Resources announced a BHEM survey and reconnaissance at its North Rock copper–nickel–PGE project in Ontario, targeting historic holes around the Beaver Pond zone. The work aims to identify new sulphide conductors beneath shallow drilling. It is exploration activity with no immediate production or market flow impact.

A Singapore businessman, accused of running a S$1.46 billion nickel investment scam, allegedly promised profits from buying and selling physical nickel but prosecutors say no nickel trades occurred. Instead, earlier investors were paid with new funds. The case centers on fraud, money laundering, and lavish personal spending.

A Singapore trial describes a $1.5 billion “nickel trading” scam in which 947 investors were duped after claims that nickel could be bought at a discount from an Australian mine and resold at profits. Prosecutors say no nickel was bought or sold; payments to earlier investors came from new money. | Minnesota’s DNR is postponing a decision on permits for NewRange Copper Nickel’s proposed copper and nickel mine on the Iron Range. The state has paused consideration until August next year due to regulatory and legal challenges, leaving output uncertain and potentially constraining future supply expectations. | Minnesota’s DNR is postponing its permit decision on the proposed NewRange Copper Nickel project in northeastern Minnesota, pausing further consideration until August next year. The delay stems from regulatory and legal challenges, potentially pushing back future copper and nickel supply. | Indonesia is seeking investors to fund delayed bauxite downstreaming and smelter development, after earlier plans were hindered by financing and several smelter projects were postponed. With a June 2023 ban on raw bauxite ore exports and only four of 12 smelters ready, the push to accelerate processing may support aluminium supply expectations.

Western Australia’s nickel industry has “collapsed” this year with multiple mine closures and job losses, attributed to cheaper Indonesian nickel flooding global markets. Analysts expect nickel to remain oversupplied until at least 2029. While green/nickel-cobalt projects continue, the current market dynamics point to weak pricing near term.

VR Resources says drilling has begun at its Empire copper–gold project in Ontario, targeting strong IP (chargeability) anomalies identified by a recent DCIP survey and related EM conductivity features. Results are preliminary and no resource or production changes are disclosed, limiting near-term implications mainly to copper sentiment.

CaNickel extended an option agreement with Cobalt One and Blackstone to purchase the Bucko Lake nickel sulfide mine, shifting the option deadline to January 3, 2025. If exercised and approvals are obtained, the buyers would advance permitting and development toward commercial production, improving nickel project outlook despite current market challenges. | SMM highlights Indonesia’s potential RKAB quota policy refinements and tighter supervision of environmental/compliance rules, alongside SIMBARA-driven application/approval delays that could affect Q1 supply. It also notes execution risks from weather and uncertainty over sustained nickel ore inflows from the Philippines, affecting supply tightness for next year.

Lifezone Metals reported a December 2024 mineral resource update at its Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, upgrading high-confidence Measured and Indicated resources and adding contained nickel and copper. The release improves resource confidence and discusses potential partner/offtake, but it is not a production or reserve change. | Lifezone Metals announced an upgrade of measured and indicated mineral resources at its Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, adding contained nickel and reporting higher-confidence resources. The resource update also includes associated copper grades. Because it does not change near-term mine supply or production, any effect on copper and nickel prices should be modest. | Lifezone Metals announced a December 2024 Mineral Resource Update for its Kabanga project in Tanzania, upgrading Measured and Indicated resources with higher contained nickel and copper grades, plus a revised nickel-equivalent cut-off. The release supports longer-term project value but provides no near-term production or supply impact. | Canada Nickel reported assay results from drill BAN24-20 at its Bannockburn nickel sulphide project, including 1.11% nickel over 10.1 metres (and up to 1.32% Ni over 4.4 metres). The company is continuing follow-up drilling and BHEM surveys, but no production or supply changes are announced. | Interros, Nornickel’s largest shareholder, objected to Rusal’s London lawsuit aimed at influencing Nornickel’s dividend policy. The dispute could force sensitive disclosures under geopolitical conditions. Nornickel is a major nickel producer, while Rusal uses dividends to service debt; however, no immediate production or trade changes are cited. | Ford is positioning for EV growth by investing in Indonesia, the world’s largest nickel producer. The move is linked to a $4.5 billion nickel processing project with Vale and Huayou, targeting battery cathode supply. Indonesia also pushes higher local-content rules for EVs, reinforcing nickel demand expectations.

Power Nickel says shareholders and courts approved the spin-out of its Golden Ivan and certain Chilean exploration assets into Chilean Metals Inc., with dividends payable in the new shares. It also reports drilling success at the Nisk Project, extending the Lion Zone 550 meters along strike, and adds a strategic advisor. | High-grade NPI prices (8–12%) fell to 952.2 yuan/mtu and Indonesian NPI FOB declined. Weak stainless steel spot and seasonal surplus weigh on NPI demand, keeping refined nickel rangebound amid limited supportive signals. The NPI vs refined nickel discount narrowed slightly, but overall nickel sentiment remains pressured. | Marvel Discovery Corp. says it is returning to Ontario’s Blackfly gold project and Quebec’s Duhamel nickel-copper-cobalt project, discontinuing uranium options. The release cites historical Ni/Cu/Co mineralization and corridor potential but provides no production or definitive near-term supply changes, implying limited immediate impact on copper/nickel prices.

GT Resources reported 2024 drill results from the Canalask Nickel-Copper Project in Yukon, including copper-rich skarn-like mineralization and nickel intersections (e.g., up to ~2.4% Ni and ~1.84% Cu in sections). The company frames results as expanding exploration targets, with no immediate production impact. | GT Resources reported final 2024 drill results at its Yukon Canalask nickel-copper project, including copper-rich and nickel-rich intercepts (skarn-like Cu-Au and peridotite-hosted Ni with PGE anomalism). The findings expand geological upside but do not imply near-term production changes, limiting impact on short-term copper and nickel prices. | Benton Resources reports recommenced drilling at its Great Burnt copper deposit and expands the South Pond gold-copper zone to over 2.3 km, including sulphide intersections with assays partly pending. It also describes a nickel target from an ultramafic anomaly with limited rock sampling. Overall, this is exploratory and small scale. | Norilsk Nickel expects a ~150,000 tonne nickel market surplus in 2024-2025, mainly in high-grade nickel, driven by new Indonesia capacity and China cathode nickel inflows, alongside growing exchange stocks. The company also notes many producers are unprofitable at current prices, implying further selling pressure unless demand tightens quickly.

Indonesia’s Energy and Mineral Resources Ministry said it plans further limits on nickel product output to avoid oversupply and keep prices higher, while protecting domestic miners. It did not specify timing or the exact restriction terms, but the measure could tighten global nickel supply given Indonesia’s market dominance. | Canada Nickel reported positive exploration drill results from Mann West and Mann South, including nickel intercepts above 0.3% over multiple intervals and a new discovery. The company also noted anomalous PGM horizons and overall drilling progress on 14 properties, targeting additional resources in 2025. | ING flags persistent downside risks for nickel, citing Indonesia-driven supply growth after the 2020 ore export ban, a continuing global surplus, and rising LME Class 1 stocks with more Chinese/Indonesian deliveries. Demand headwinds include weaker stainless steel activity and slower EV/battery growth, keeping prices under pressure. | Canada Nickel Company reports positive exploration and assay updates from drilling at the Mann West/Central/South properties in Ontario’s Timmins Nickel District. The program intersected nickel sulphide mineralization (pentlandite/heazlewoodite) and is expected to support a first resource estimate in Q1 2025, boosting nickel supply expectations.

Grid Metals and Teck signed an option/JV agreement to explore the Makwa Ni-Cu-PGM-cobalt project in Manitoba. Teck can earn up to 70% by funding exploration expenditures and cash payments, with an initial drilling campaign anticipated. The update may influence sentiment for nickel and copper, but not near-term supply. | Barksdale Resources said its Sunnyside drilling in Arizona intercepted copper-dominant semi-massive sulfides in a CRD-style target, including chalcopyrite-rich intervals, with drilling continuing deeper. Assays are pending, but the copper-rich discovery and proximity to known mineralized districts could support near-term copper sentiment. | EV Nickel reports QEMSCAN/EPMA results for a new zone confirming nickel sulphides (millerite and heazlewoodite) that could improve recovery rates and produce higher-grade nickel concentrate, with chalcopyrite also present as a dominant copper mineral. The company plans follow-up metallurgical flotation tests and 2025 drilling to define a resource.

The piece discusses copper market fundamentals and focuses on BHP as a top copper stock for hedge funds. It cites copper surplus/supply and China demand headwinds, but highlights BHP’s rising copper output and a new Argentina copper joint venture, which supports near-term copper sentiment. | Grid Metals signed an option/JV agreement with Teck Resources to develop the Makwa nickel project in Manitoba. Teck may earn up to 70% by funding C$15.7m in expenditures with staged cash payments and further exploration commitments. The deal may support future nickel supply, but near-term price impact is likely limited. | Canada Nickel says Taykwa Tagamou Nation will invest $20 million via convertible notes, potentially taking an 8.3% equity stake, and gaining a board seat for its Crawford nickel-cobalt project. The funding reduces uncertainty amid junior mining capital crunches and supports expectations for future nickel supply, modestly bullish for nickel.

SMM reports stable nickel ore pricing this week: Philippine FOB prices around $29/wmt for 1.3% ore, and Indonesia premiums $15–17/wmt. However, declining high-grade NPI prices in China and high restocking costs add downward pressure. Indonesian policy rumors on restricting output may offer limited support. | Taykwa Tagamou Nation will invest $20 million via a convertible note in Canada Nickel, taking an 8.4% interest in the company. Proceeds support the Crawford Nickel Sulphide Project, with conversion expected in January 2025. The deal reinforces development momentum for a major Canadian nickel mine. | Mink Ventures announced the start of a diamond drill program at its Warren Ni-Cu-Co project in Ontario, targeting geophysical anomalies at the Shaft, North and SW zones. The zones show prior copper and nickel mineralization associated with IP/EM responses. The work is intended to maintain momentum toward earning full ownership; no production changes are reported. | Mink Ventures began diamond drilling at its Warren project in Timmins, Ontario, targeting geophysical anomalies associated with nickel, copper, and cobalt mineralization. The company highlights prior massive sulphide and disseminated sulphide assays at the North, Shaft, and SW zones, aiming to test untested IP/EM targets. | Taykwa Tagamou Nation (TTN) will invest $20 million via a convertible note into Canada Nickel’s Crawford nickel sulphide project, taking an 8.4% equity interest and a board nomination right. The financing is expected to close in January 2025, supporting continued development toward construction decisions. | Canada Nickel announced it has secured $20 million in financing from Ontario’s Tagamou Nation as part of a new partnership tied to the company’s advancing mine construction. By strengthening funding for development progress, the news can reduce perceived near-term project risk and be mildly supportive for nickel markets. | The piece argues Indonesia is ramping nickel output, expanding LME and SHFE exchange inventories substantially (with limited price support). Coupled with soft stainless demand, buyer reluctance to forward purchase, and ongoing downside bias in the NAS 304 surcharge, it points to bearish near-term nickel price pressure.

First Atlantic Nickel said it will acquire 100% of the RPM-South claims adjacent to its RPM nickel discovery in Newfoundland, enabling further exploration for depth and extensions. It resumes Phase 1 core processing and has submitted Phase 2 drilling permits. The release also promotes awaruite’s processing advantages for nickel supply security. | Taykwa Tagamou Nation will invest C$28.6m via a convertible note to advance Canada Nickel’s Crawford nickel sulphide project in Ontario. The partners plan to support development in the Timmins Nickel District as the project moves toward a construction decision, potentially improving near-term nickel supply expectations.

Westwin Elements announced first successful production of nickel powder at its Lawton, Oklahoma demonstration plant, designed to make about 20 tons per year via a carbonyl process. The company plans to produce Class 1 samples for customer qualifications to support a more domestic nickel supply chain, reducing foreign dependence. | NorthX Nickel announced an agreement with Magna Mining to sell its Sudbury portfolio of nickel and base metal assets. The transaction streamlines NorthX toward its Grasset nickel project, while Magna assumes related liabilities and operator/royalty obligations. Closure is expected by end of Q1 2025, subject to approvals. | Nickel 28 reported results for the quarter ended Oct. 31, 2024. Its Ramu nickel-cobalt JV produced and sold contained nickel, but a mechanical failure shut one acid plant, cutting production capacity roughly in half until end-February 2025, lowering 2025 nickel-in-MHP guidance to ~29,000 tonnes.

Indonesia is reportedly weighing deep cuts to nickel mining quotas, potentially reducing ore mined next year to about 150 million tons from 227 million this year. The move aims to support nickel prices amid smelter complaints of ore shortages and higher reliance on imports. | The report’s headline says Indonesia is considering deep cuts to nickel mining to support higher prices. By signaling reduced near-term nickel supply, the move would likely tighten market expectations and provide upward pressure on nickel prices over the coming days to weeks.  | A Trump victory is expected to loosen U.S. and state constraints on Minnesota mining. The piece highlights efforts to restart Twin Metals’ copper-nickel project and advance NewRange’s long-stalled PolyMet-era mine via renewed permitting. Environmental groups warn of renewed legal challenges, but policy winds could aid timelines. | Minnesota mining firms are expecting a Trump administration to reverse the Biden-era Superior National Forest mining moratorium and reduce permitting friction. Twin Metals hopes to restart copper-nickel mining, while NewRange Copper Nickel may reapply for required federal and state permits. Environmental opponents warn of renewed court battles.

The article links nickel’s 2024 price plunge to Indonesia’s outsized role in global nickel supply and its competitive, low-cost production, alongside alleged environmental and labor issues. It cites closures or imminent shutdowns at mines and smelters and Vale job cuts due to falling nickel benchmarks, indicating bearish near-term sentiment.

Indonesia is considering limiting nickel ore production to stabilize international prices amid oversupply and weaker demand. Officials cite oversupply as a key driver of falling nickel prices and note recent declines linked to demand weakness in stainless steel and battery markets. Timing is not specified, but supply restraint is the core policy signal.

Indonesia’s Energy and Mineral Resources Ministry said it has not yet discussed plans to curb next year’s nickel production quota, though companies were reportedly weighing deeper cuts. The statement points to ongoing uncertainty rather than immediate supply restraint. Nickel prices have been falling amid a stronger dollar, weak demand, and ample supply.

West Australia’s downturn in lithium and nickel has led to mine closures and reduced market value for local producers. At the same time, the labour layoffs appear to benefit gold miners, as staff availability and strong silver demand improve hiring and output prospects. The nickel impact is via shuttered production.

Tartisan Nickel Corp. completed a 149-line km airborne geophysical survey over its Turtle Pond nickel-related claims and closed a $500,000 financing at $0.18 per unit. Proceeds will fund exploration and development of its Kenbridge Nickel Project. No near-term production, inventory, or trade impacts are indicated.

Prices for high-grade nickel pig iron (NPI) fell (SMM and Indonesian FOB). Rising Indonesian supply and reduced stainless steel spot demand during maintenance weaken purchasing, while smelter losses from ore-driven cash costs lead to output adjustments. Pure nickel faces macro headwinds and underperforming demand, keeping nickel bearish. | SMM reports Indonesian laterite nickel ore CIF/FOB premiums stable-to-weak while policy signals remain awaited. High-grade NPI prices are continuing to decline with sluggish transactions, despite rising December output. Smelters have over two months of inventory and refined nickel production is ramping up, supporting expectations of nickel oversupply.

Nickel prices are slumping as EV demand expectations weaken and oversupply persists, with Chinese firms promoting nickel-free LFP batteries instead of nickel-based chemistries. At the same time, Harum Energy’s Indonesian projects expand nickel refining capacity, which could further weigh on near-term nickel balances.

Sumitomo Metal Mining will buy the remaining 15.625% stake in the Philippines’ Coral Bay Nickel HPAL smelter from Nickel Asia, raising its ownership to 100%. The plant has 24,000 t/y nickel capacity. The move strengthens SMM’s nickel processing footprint, likely supporting nickel supply expectations in the near term. | Canada Nickel announced agreements to create ExploreCo and secure surface rights for its Crawford nickel sulphide project near Timmins, Ontario. It consolidates mining and access rights to streamline development and permitting toward a 2025 construction decision. The transactions are subject to TSX Venture Exchange approvals. | Philippine mining officials expect stronger 2025 demand for copper and nickel as EVs and renewable-energy technologies scale. Copper output and production value rose sharply in the first nine months of 2024, while nickel output fell year on year, suggesting potential supply tightness. Net-zero policy supports demand outlook despite governance uncertainty. | Philippine mining officials expect EV and renewable-energy growth to raise 2025 demand for nickel and copper. They cite falling nickel ore output earlier but improved metallic production overall, with copper output up sharply in 2024’s first nine months. Net-zero policy support is framed as a continuing catalyst. | Nickel Asia Corp., the Philippines’ largest nickel producer, said it is negotiating with Sumitomo Metal Mining to sell its entire stake in Coral Bay Nickel Corp., which operates an HPAL plant. The company will reclassify the investment and stop recognizing equity gains or losses, but the deal’s impact on near-term supply is unclear.

Nickel Asia plans to sell its entire 15.63% stake in Coral Bay Nickel to Sumitomo, retaining a 10% interest in Taganito. Coral Bay is the Philippines’ sole HPAL processing plant. The move follows equity losses linked to higher costs and lower LME nickel prices, amid a nickel glut.

Indonesia is weighing cuts to nickel mine quotas for 2025, potentially reducing output from 272 mln to 150 mln tons. Macquarie said such cuts are unlikely, but if implemented they could remove up to 35% of global nickel supply, creating upside risk for prices amid prior Fitch downgrades. | The public can comment until Feb. 7 on Canada Nickel’s Crawford Nickel Project environmental impact statement, covering a long-running open-pit nickel-and-cobalt mine. The assessment flags habitat and water impacts and mitigation measures. It’s in phase 2 of a 5-phase process, with decisions still far off.

Indonesia’s mining ministry set a nickel ore mining quota of around 200 million metric tons for 2025. The quota may be reduced if miners fail performance checks, especially regarding reclamation and environmental management. A higher quota generally supports nickel supply expectations, weighing on prices near term. | Macquarie warns that proposed Indonesian nickel mine quota cuts could slash output from 272mt in 2024 to about 150mt, removing more than one-third of global supply. Even if a major cut is unlikely, lower-than-expected production is seen as an upside risk for nickel prices. | High-nickel pig iron prices in China and Indonesia weakened, with SMM NPI falling weekly and the Indonesian FOB index declining. Stainless steel maintenance reduced procurement, while Indonesia’s new capacity and supply variability increased. The NPI discount to electrolytic nickel widened, and macro/FX pressure plus possible oversupply sustain bearish nickel sentiment. | SMM reviews Indonesia’s nickel market: nickel ore domestic trade improved amid policy uncertainty, with supply stabilizing and ore premiums potentially rising. However, Indonesian high-grade NPI FOB fell $0.7/mtu WoW, with seasonal stainless demand weakness. Refined nickel production is ramping up, with more output expected in 2025.

Abaxx plans to launch a physically deliverable lithium carbonate contract within weeks after starting trading of its nickel sulphate futures. The exchange’s physically deliverable nickel contract begins in Singapore, with settlement linked to the LME nickel price and possible new delivery points. The story focuses on contract mechanics rather than nickel fundamentals. | MetalMiner reports bearish nickel market conditions, with Indonesia blamed for a supply glut. Nickel prices fell below $15k/mt to a multi-year low and exchange inventories rose sharply (LME and SHFE at highs). Indonesia is considering cutting mining quotas, but tighter supply may not be immediate, keeping prices under pressure. | Reuters notes the LME has fully recovered from its 2022 nickel crisis, with nickel inventories rising sharply and trading volumes rebounding. The exchange expanded approved nickel brands and broader stock/liquidity trends supported higher activity across base metals, while copper and aluminium are referenced largely as part of the overall inventory cycle.

Reuters says the LME has fully recovered from the 2022 nickel crisis, with 2024 trading volumes surging and LME nickel inventory rising to around 230,000 mt by end-November 2024 from under 40,000 in May 2023. Higher stocks and improved delivery options point to easing tightness. | Pan Global plans to accelerate 2025 exploration in Spain, including imminent drilling at the Escacena and CaÌrmenes projects. The program targets copper-tin-silver extensions at La Romana and copper-cobalt-nickel mineralization at CaÌrmenes. No immediate mine output or trade disruption is cited. | Reuters reports the LME has fully recovered from the 2022 nickel crisis, with nickel inventories rising to nearly 230,000 tonnes and new nickel brands approved, boosting confidence. It also cites higher exchange stocks across base metals (including aluminium) and fluctuating copper volumes tied to fund flows, implying mixed near-term price signals.

Hannan Metals reports a 1.2 km induced-polarization chargeability anomaly at its Sortilegio copper-gold porphyry prospect in Peru and states the target is drill-ready with permits for Q2 2025. This is positive for long-term copper optionality, but it does not affect current supply or inventories. | First Atlantic Nickel says Newfoundland’s Mineral Lands Division has granted permits for Phase 2 drilling and a southern access trail at its Atlantic Nickel Project. The program will expand the RPM discovery area and target extensions toward Chrome Pond and Pipestone. The firm is preparing for drilling and expects Phase 1 assay updates soon.

The article argues that Indonesia’s rising nickel output is driving higher deforestation, pollution, and mining-related disasters in Sulawesi. It warns that weak sustainability practices increase social and environmental risk, potentially leading to stricter oversight or cost burdens. The study suggests near-term sentiment risk for nickel. | Terrain Minerals has begun reverse-circulation drilling at its Lort River copper-nickel project, targeting Nova-Bollinger-style sulphide conductors based on EM data. The company expects RC drilling to finish in late January and assays in March, but the report is exploratory with no immediate supply effects for nickel or copper prices. | Port Kembla Copper has sold its former smelter site to developers, enabling rehabilitation of about 300 nearby homes after inspections found historical heavy-metal contamination. The smelter operated from 2000 to closure in 2003, and remediation work will begin post-sale. The news is localized and does not change current metal supply. | The piece argues that nickel mining in Indonesia’s Sulawesi is linked to nearly double deforestation rates near mines (2011–2018), plus higher pollution and mining-related disasters like landslides and flash floods. It frames these harms as potential drivers of stricter oversight and disruption risk for nickel operations, impacting supply expectations. | Zambia’s mines minister says Saudi Manara Minerals is exploring investments in the country’s critical-minerals projects, including copper and nickel assets, with discussions already held and a potential deal likely this year. The government also seeks up to 30% stakes in new projects and is expanding copper trading arrangements, boosting revenue expectations. | Petrosea won a $1bn contract from Vale Indonesia to mine nickel ore in Central Sulawesi, with a scheduled March signing and 10-year term. The deal sits within Indonesia’s ongoing restructuring of nickel investment, including HPAL capacity plans and potential battery-grade partnerships.

Ardea Resources is advancing the $98.5 million definitive feasibility study for its Goongarrie Hub nickel-laterite project in Western Australia, with multiple rigs and contracts for metallurgical and water studies. The DFS targets economics for long-term production, but it remains pre-FID with updates expected later in 2025. | Korea Zinc said its nickel sulfate manufacturing technology for cathode metal compounds was designated a South Korean national strategic technology. The move provides government tax and administrative support and supports investment in a large all-in-one nickel refinery planned to start in 2026, reducing costs and improving nickel supply readiness. | Global nickel production is expected to fall 7.4% in 2024 to about 3.4 million tonnes as major producers cut output amid declining prices and operational disruptions. Indonesia, the Philippines, and New Caledonia face notable declines, with regulatory approval delays contributing to reduced mine output. | SMM’s weekly Indonesia-1.17 nickel market review says Indonesian domestic laterite nickel ore premiums generally fluctuated higher, with ample supply and smelter inventories above two weeks. Demand improved on quota/stockpiling effects. NPI prices rebounded, losses eased, and nickel ore CIF prices are expected stable with an upward trend into the next weeks. | A US- and EU-backed Lobito Corridor rail project linking Angola’s Lobito port with the DRC and Zambia aims to speed shipments and expand freight capacity. It notes current copper concentrate flows from Ivanhoe Mines and expects much higher train throughput, supporting copper logistics. Nickel-linked investments are mentioned but are less immediate. | South Korea designated Korea Zinc’s nickel sulphate manufacturing technology as a national strategic technology, expanding eligible support and tax deductions. The company plans to start full-scale operations at a >500bn won all-in-one nickel refinery in 2026, aiming for up to 90bn won cost savings. The move strengthens domestic battery nickel supply competitiveness. | The opinion argues that mining consolidation may resume, with major buyers focused on copper assets such as Rio Tinto/BHP targets and Anglo’s Collahuasi. It emphasizes a global copper shortage from electrification, implying tighter supply. It also notes BHP’s plan to mothball nickel operations, potentially supporting nickel sentiment.

Anglo American plans to sell its Brazil nickel assets, Barro Alto and Niquelandia, by H1 2025 as part of a restructuring after BHP’s takeover bid. Analysts expect obtaining a good price will be hard given a weak nickel price, implying continued bearish sentiment for nickel.

BHP said copper production rose 17% year-on-year to 510,700 tonnes in the second quarter, with Chile’s Escondida delivering a 22% increase to a ten-year record. However, guidance for copper South Australia was lowered after a weather-related power outage. BHP also noted a USD300m impact from WA nickel following temporary suspension. | Noronex reported multiple strong copper-silver drill intercepts at its Fiesta project in Namibia, including 16m at 1.3% copper equivalent and other wider, higher-grade hits. The company also highlights silver’s potential to improve concentrate payability. Overall, the news is positive for copper sentiment but unlikely to affect near-term market prices materially. | Infinico Metals announced the start of a fixed loop fluxgate electromagnetic survey at its Nicobi Ni-Cu-Co property in Québec to refine conductive targets for future drilling. The company cites prior drilling results showing nickel-copper sulphide mineralization, but no production or operational changes are reported. | Infinico Metals began a fixed-loop fluxgate surface electromagnetic survey at its Nicobi property to refine conductive targets for potential Ni–Cu sulphide drilling. Prior drilling at Showing A intersected nickel and copper mineralization, but follow-up closed it off. The release focuses on exploration targeting rather than production or supply changes. | Alaska Energy Metals reported 2024 exploration results at its Angliers-Belleterre project in Quebec, outlining multiple undrilled VMS and Cu-Ni targets with anomalous copper and nickel in soil, rock, and VTEM results. The company plans further geophysical modeling and diamond drilling to test mineralization at depth. | Fathom Nickel reported 2024 exploration results expanding the historic Gochager Lake nickel sulphide footprint and validating magmatic mineralization at Albert Lake, plus plans for further EM surveys and drilling in 2025. The company expects improving nickel prices and notes Indonesia production curbs, but no near-term supply disruption occurs. | Indonesia’s mineral authorities are considering cutting domestic nickel production so supply better matches domestic and export needs and avoids further sustained price declines. As the world’s leading nickel miner, Indonesia’s potential quota reductions could tighten supply expectations and support international nickel prices, amid recent sharp losses.

Reuters column says Indonesia’s nickel sector has flooded markets and drove nickel to four-year lows, but Jakarta plans to cut 2025 ore mining quotas to 200 Mt from 240 Mt. The quota outlook has coincided with a modest LME 3-month nickel rebound (+3% since early January), though inventories remain elevated. | Trump’s administration launched an “America First Trade Policy” investigation under Section 232 focused on imported steel and aluminum. The review assesses exclusions/exemptions and could lead to adjusted tariffs or import restrictions after scheduled reports. The announcement is conditional and timing is uncertain, limiting immediate directional impact.

Reuters columnist says Indonesia is pivotal for nickel’s recovery after 2024’s price bust. The Indonesian mining ministry plans to cut nickel ore quotas to 200m tons from a planned 240m, helping lift LME 3-month nickel by ~3% since early January, though rising inventories and weaker EV battery demand limit follow-through. | The Trump administration has launched an investigation under Section 232 into whether to adjust tariff restrictions on imported steel and aluminum, reviewing exclusions and potential new import limitations tied to national security. Prior 232 tariffs included 10% aluminum in many cases, with possible further changes.

Canada Nickel announced positive exploration drill results from its Midlothian and Deloro properties, with multiple holes intersecting shallow, mineralized ultramafic rocks and reporting nickel grades (e.g., 0.27% over 371m including 0.30% over 39m at Deloro). It also describes ongoing infill work at Nesbitt. This supports bullish nickel sentiment for the near term. | SMM reports Indonesia’s 2025 RKAB quota for nickel ore at 298 million wmt and active ore trading, with smelters starting February procurement. High-grade NPI FOB/inland prices recovered on tighter circulation ahead of Chinese New Year, but quota approval and rising LME nickel inventories restrained refined nickel gains, implying mixed near-term direction. | High-nickel pig iron prices increased this week, with the Indonesian NPI FOB index also rising. Supply was slightly tightened by weaker domestic production due to smelter losses, while tight circulating resources and long-term contract coverage supported demand. The NPI-to-electrolytic nickel discount narrowed, implying buoyant nickel sentiment. | COBCO commissioned its first batch of NCM precursor cathode active materials lines in Jorf Lasfar, Morocco, targeting 120kT/year PCAM capacity. The NCM (nickel-containing) production ramp-up and planned closed-loop recycling expansion point to higher near-term demand for nickel materials used in battery cathodes. | An environmental group says Canada Nickel’s proposed Crawford Project could harm freshwater by releasing effluent into the North Driftwood River, a relatively small stream. The group is reviewing the large impact statement and has concerns about long-term effects over a 41-year mine life, prompting ongoing scrutiny.

Core Nickel Corp. published its 2024 year-end review and outlines 2025 exploration plans for its Thompson Nickel Belt projects, including planned VTEM, re-sampling, and a 4,000–5,000 m drill program at the Mel and Halfway Lake areas. The update targets resource expansion and does not affect near-term nickel supply directly.

Terra Metals says it will re-commission its copper and cobalt acid leach plant by end-Q1 2025 and commission a 1 million tpa flotation plant by end-Q2 2025 in Zambia. It also plans 400 MW solar capacity to power operations. The expansion improves copper production outlook and supports bullish near-term pricing.

Core Nickel Corp. started helicopter-borne VTEM electromagnetic surveys on its Mel and Odei River nickel projects in Manitoba and plans further drilling after survey results. The news updates exploration targeting deeper nickel sulphide potential, with no changes to production, exports, or supply affecting near-term nickel markets. | Tanzania and Burundi signed an agreement with two Chinese firms to build a $2.15 billion railway connecting the two countries and carrying metals, including battery-mineral nickel, to Dar es Salaam port. The 282 km line is expected to move about three million tonnes of minerals annually, improving nickel transport capacity.

A report and experts warn that expanding nickel mining in Raja Ampat, Indonesia, is damaging a UNESCO-protected marine region. Satellite data show mining pit area grew sharply since 2020. Despite a March 2024 court ruling requiring extra protection for small islands, permits continue, raising risks to nickel production timelines. | Glencore reported 2024 output declines across several commodities, including copper. Copper production fell to 951,600 tonnes, down 6% from 2023 and at the low end of its guidance. Management expects to provide 2025 guidance in February, with improved H2 performance supporting some offset. | Burundi and Tanzania agreed with Chinese firms to build a $2.15bn 282 km railway to Dar es Salaam to move minerals, including battery nickel. Financed with African Development Bank support, the line is expected to haul three million tonnes annually. Improved export logistics could lift near-term expectations for nickel supply. | A report says nickel mining expansion in Indonesia’s Raja Ampat region has rapidly increased land use and raised fears of deforestation and sediment pollution harming coral ecosystems.
