Although mining is still operating, growing scrutiny and backlash around nickel projects could threaten near-term supply continuity, supporting nickel prices. | NorthX Nickel Corp. announced a non-brokered subscription receipt financing to fund general working capital and to support a proposed acquisition of its Sudbury Basin nickel and base-metal assets. The escrow release depends on transaction conditions. The update is corporate/financing-focused with no direct production or pricing details, implying limited near-term nickel price effect.

The Nickel Institute comments on the EU “Competitiveness Compass,” arguing that EU regulatory inconsistencies—particularly revisions to nickel environmental quality standards proposed under the Water Framework Directive—could harm producers by constraining investment and competitiveness. It calls for streamlined permitting, reduced regulatory burden, and affordable energy to sustain EU nickel supply for batteries and the energy transition.

Reuters column citing a January base-metals analyst poll expects aluminium to lead in 2025 on a projected supply deficit, with cash prices set to rise. Copper upside is tempered by tariff-driven macro risk, especially for China demand. Nickel remains bearish on ongoing oversupply and rising stocks, limiting near-term net impact.

The article argues that rapid EV and renewable-storage growth is tightening global battery-metal supply chains. It highlights strong demand for nickel sulfate for NCM/NCA cathodes and notes irregular supply from producers such as Indonesia and the Philippines, alongside environmental and production-quote constraints. It implies continued nickel price volatility and upside bias. | The piece frames the energy transition as long-run support for copper, aluminum, and nickel and cites SMM’s January 20, 2025 price levels. It notes copper premiums weakening ahead of Chinese New Year due to softer consumption, while nickel premiums fall on abundant supply and reduced activity, implying mixed short-term pressure. | A report by C4ADS says Chinese firms control about 75% of Indonesia’s nickel refining capacity, dominated by Tsingshan and Delong. It raises concerns over supply-chain control, environmental risk, and possible disadvantages for automakers amid tighter U.S./EU trade policies. Indonesia’s response is not detailed, implying no immediate supply shock. | A New York judge allowed Trafigura to subpoena records from major banks in a nickel-related fraud case to trace where funds paid for purported nickel cargoes were transferred. The underlying claim is that worthless material was shipped instead, but the story contains no direct supply or inventory impact.

A report says Chinese firms control about 75% of Indonesia’s nickel refining capacity, with Tsingshan and Jiangsu Delong owning over 70%. Concerns focus on supply-chain resilience and possible disruptions from restrictive trade policies. Reuters also notes Chinese moves to cut stakes via partnerships and Indonesia’s push for more domestic control. | Podium Minerals completed the acquisition of EV Nickel’s Range Well nickel project, integrating it with its Parks Reef PGM project in Western Australia. The combined assets also reportedly include copper alongside nickel and cobalt. The deal involves issuing shares and a royalty to Johnson Matthey, but no near-term production impact is specified. | Fastmarkets’ technical note argues LME nickel’s bearish setup may be temporary. It describes a potential inverse head-and-shoulders pattern with a neckline near $16,000/t and suggests that only a monthly close below $14,500/t would invalidate the bullish bias. Otherwise, it expects a limited rebound/short-covering rally in coming weeks.

High-nickel pig iron (NPI) prices rose this week, with SMM 8–12% NPI averaging 944.2 RMB per nickel point and the NPI FOB index also increasing. Resumed smelter operations, limited Indonesian/Philippine output, and a Philippines raw-ore export ban strengthened sentiment, narrowing the discount to electrolytic nickel. | SMM reports Indonesian domestic trade laterite nickel ore activity rebounded post-holiday, with premiums rising (1.6 grade $17–18) amid ample supply and February restocking by pyrometallurgical plants. High-grade NPI prices strengthened, with the FOB index up WoW and improved smelter economics, supported by positive sentiment and export-ban news.

Trump announced a 25% tariff on all steel and aluminium imports to the US, likely including Australia, prompting diplomatic efforts for an exemption. The piece notes aluminium export volumes and likely competitive pressure on manufacturers. The tariff threat is a near-term negative for aluminium price expectations; copper and nickel aren’t directly addressed. | Asian markets were mixed, but LME base metals mostly rose after President Trump pledged 25% tariffs on all U.S. steel and aluminum imports. Aluminum futures gained 0.6% and copper 1.2% on the day, while nickel fell 0.5%. Analysts expected limited broader impact on emerging Asia exports. | Trump’s proposed 25% tariffs on all U.S. imports of steel and aluminium are rattling Asian producers by threatening higher U.S. import prices, disrupted trade flows, and lower volumes. Reports cite aluminium supply-tightening concerns and falling iron-ore sentiment, while copper and nickel are not directly addressed. | Trump says the US will impose a 25% tariff on steel and aluminium imports, with details expected soon. Australia’s industry urges a carve-out, citing past exemptions. Even with exemptions, the risk is weaker demand if tariffs slow China, pressuring overall industrial metal sentiment, especially aluminium. | Trump said the US will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminium imports starting this week, with further “reciprocal” measures. The tariff backdrop includes potential retaliation from China and warnings that tariffs will raise US costs, likely pressuring near-term industrial demand and sentiment for copper, nickel, and aluminium. | Trump signed executive orders imposing a flat 25% tariff on all steel and aluminum imports into the US “without exceptions.” The piece also discusses possible US consideration of tariff exemptions for Australia and European warnings of retaliatory tariffs. Overall, aluminum faces direct near-term price support from higher import costs. | Donald Trump says the US will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminium imports, including from Canada and Mexico, and is planning reciprocal tariffs almost immediately. The move escalates trade policy and could trigger retaliation, likely pressuring industrial metals demand expectations and prices near term. | Trump said the U.S. will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and “aluminum, too” imports, including from Canada and Mexico, with additional reciprocal duties later. Markets fell and partners warned of industrial impacts. Aluminum-focused expectations likely deteriorate near term; copper and nickel only face indirect macro/trade pressure. | Trump has signed executive orders imposing a flat 25% tariff on all US steel and aluminum imports “without exceptions or exemptions.” The news covers potential tariff exemptions for Australia, expected limited impact comments from ThyssenKrupp, and EU retaliation threats—driving near-term expectations for aluminum trade costs and prices, with only indirect effects for copper and nickel. | Trump announced executive actions raising US tariffs on aluminium and steel to 25% from 10% as China’s levies against the US take effect. Canada is the dominant source of primary aluminium imports to the US, while Mexico supplies much of the aluminium scrap and alloy. Higher tariffs likely pressure aluminium markets near term. | Trump plans to announce 25% tariffs on all imported steel and aluminum entering the U.S., including shipments from Canada and Mexico. Canada supplies major steel and is the top aluminum metal source. The move follows prior tariff threats and could tighten U.S. aluminum availability, but details remain limited. | Trump said 25% tariffs will be imposed on all steel and aluminium imports starting Monday, including shipments from Canada and Mexico. He referenced earlier 2018 tariffs and national security claims, as well as the 2019 deal that previously prevented supply surges. The new tariffs may raise costs and prices for aluminium. | Trump removed exemptions from U.S. steel tariffs so all steel imports face at least 25%, and raised 2018 aluminum tariffs to 25% from 10%. The policy change is meant to protect domestic industry and reset trade, likely increasing aluminum import costs and near-term price support. Other updates are unrelated to metal prices. | Norilsk Nickel said its 2024 IFRS net profit fell 37% to $1.8 bln as EBITDA declined and revenues dropped 13% amid weaker market prices for nickel and platinum group metals. The company reported lower free cash flow and higher net debt, while continuing investment and debt management plans. | Stocks in steel and aluminum producers jumped after President Trump said he plans 25% tariffs on imported steel and aluminum and will announce retaliatory duties on countries that impose tariffs on U.S. goods. Canada is a major exporter, contributing about 40% of U.S. aluminum imports. | Nornickel reported 2024 net profit down 37% to $1.8 billion, citing Western sanctions pressures and low prices for its key metals. The company noted payments/equipment access constraints, redirected sales to Asia, and faced repairs disruption at its Nadezhda smelter. It also warned it sees a nickel surplus in 2025. | Trump plans 25% tariffs on all US steel and aluminum imports, targeting China-linked trade flows and expanding an already tariff-heavy stance. The piece notes China’s overcapacity and rising exports depressing prices globally, which has triggered retaliation and further trade friction likely to pressure industrial metals pricing in the near term. | Trump signed executive orders imposing 25% tariffs on all US imports of steel and aluminum with no exceptions or exemptions. The US may also consider exemptions for Australian products. Europe and France signaled retaliatory measures. The tariff news is likely to raise near-term expectations for aluminum supply constraints in the US market. | President Trump orders 25% import duties on all steel and raises aluminium tariffs from 10% to 25%, covering about $50B of imports. Tariffs are expected to be passed through to U.S. buyers, pushing up prices for products using these metals and likely lifting nearby aluminium pricing pressure through higher import costs and substitution effects. | Trump signed executive orders imposing 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the U.S. from all countries, effective March 12, with Canada threatening “clear and calibrated” retaliation. The broad tariff scope includes downstream products and is expected to disrupt aluminum trade flows.

Trump says he will halt producing pennies. The article notes pennies have used mostly zinc since 1982, with only about 198 metric tons of copper embedded in 2024 pennies—around 0.02% of annual U.S. copper production. Any change should not meaningfully move copper prices. Nickel effects are speculative; aluminium is not mentioned. | Aluminium prices fell as investors worried about a possible global trade war after Trump imposed 25% tariffs on U.S. steel and aluminium imports. LME three-month aluminium dropped 0.6% and SHFE aluminium fell 0.5%. Copper moved modestly on tariff expectations, while nickel declined amid broader market caution. | Trump’s administration is imposing new tariffs on steel imports (25% minimum) and also includes a 25% tariff on aluminum from March 12. Industry leaders warn these duties could tighten short-term supply and raise input costs for automakers, likely supporting near-term aluminum prices, while copper and nickel impacts are not specified. | The AP reports new Trump tariffs: all steel imports face at least 25%, alongside a 25% aluminum tariff. Auto executives warn these duties will raise costs through supply-demand tightening and higher component prices. Producers may seek more capacity and face short-term supply constraints, supporting higher aluminum prices. | CNGR said it will end investment in a nickel refinery joint venture with Posco Holdings, leading to liquidation of Posco CNGR Nickel Solution. The move follows weak global EV demand and slower battery installation growth, reducing the outlook for the planned nickel sulphate and battery-precursor facility in Pohang. | The Philippines’ Senate has passed a bill to ban nickel ore exports to push downstream processing. The measure is under bicameral review and would only start five years after signing. As the second-largest laterite nickel ore exporter, any progress fuels expectations of tighter ore supply and potential support for nickel prices. | Denmark’s EIFO says it sold its exposure to Horizonte Minerals’ Brazilian nickel project after the Araguaia development failed. Management linked the collapse to plunging nickel prices in early 2024 and sharp cost overruns. EIFO removed the project from its books, emphasizing tighter monitoring for future nickel deals. | The IEA and C4ADS warn nickel supply chains are vulnerable because Indonesia’s refining capacity is heavily influenced by Chinese companies and concentrated ownership. If Indonesian refined nickel supply is disrupted, alternative sources may fall far below demand. The news raises near-term tightness concerns for nickel prices amid escalating geopolitics. | Trudeau warned that a U.S.-Canada trade war tied to Trump’s steel and aluminum tariffs could cost jobs, with duties potentially stacking to 50% for steel and additional levies including on energy and critical minerals. The Section 232 tariff escalation directly raises aluminium trade frictions and demand uncertainty in the near term. | First Quantum’s CFO said the company will not rush to sell a minority stake in its Zambian copper mines and sees no set timeline. It is seeking a long-term partner, with Manara reported in advanced talks. The move is linked to funding Kansanshi expansion, after Cobre Panama’s shutdown. | Trump announced 25% US tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports, with Canadian products facing additional stacked levies. Canada’s steelmakers and aluminum producers in places like Hamilton (Ontario) and Quebec are bracing for a major slowdown and potential retaliatory measures, raising short-term downside risk for aluminum prices. | Trudeau said Canada will respond “unequivocally” to Trump tariffs, even with stacked levies. The U.S. announced a 25% tariff on all steel and aluminum imports starting March 12, bringing steel/aluminum to 50% total. Canada plans countermeasures and cautions against trade escalation.

President Trump signed proclamations expanding Section 232 tariffs on steel articles (25%) and raising aluminum import tariffs from 10% to 25%, revoking alternative arrangements and terminating product-specific exclusions. Tariffs apply to imports from all countries effective March 12, 2025, after interim impacts, aiming to protect U.S. steel and aluminum industries. | Power Nickel priced a C$40m best-efforts private placement of Quebec flow-through shares to fund Canadian exploration. Proceeds will expand work at the Nisk nickel-copper sulphide project in Quebec, including Nisk and Lion zones and targets, supporting sentiment for future nickel and copper supply. | Trump’s proclamation expands 25% tariffs on steel and increases aluminium tariffs from 10% to 25%, applying to all countries from March 12, 2025. It also revokes prior exemptions for steel and aluminium from several allies and for various derivative products. Importers must reassess supply-chain cost impacts.

Yukon officials say the court-appointed receiver at Eagle Gold Mine has started discharging on-site melt-prep water with elevated copper that is non-compliant with water regulations. Testing found contamination reaching Haggart Creek and exceedances for cyanide, plus detections of nickel and other contaminants. The issue is being managed via settling pond construction. | St. Albert businesses are bracing for an international trade war after the U.S. imposed a 25% tariff on imported steel and aluminum. A local steel fabricator reports supplier price increases and bidding uncertainty. A brewery expects higher aluminum can and possibly other input costs, with potential Canadian retaliation risks. | President Trump issued a proclamation imposing 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the U.S. effective March 12, terminating prior exclusions and expanding to derivative products. Foreign trade zone and duty drawback benefits are limited. The breadth increases near-term trade and demand risk that can pressure industrial metals sentiment. | SMM reports Indonesian nickel ore premiums for domestic trade and the Indonesian NPI FOB index have risen, supported by restocking demand, tight available ore due to rainy-season disruptions, and reduced smelter operating load. Even with weak stainless demand, trader procurement and narrowing smelter losses keep high-grade NPI prices firm. | Trump expanded tariffs on steel and aluminum imports (25% on both, with aluminum’s rate raised), expected to raise input costs for Wisconsin manufacturers. Equipment makers estimate higher steel costs, while beverage producers note aluminum sourcing adjustments. The tariff shock implies near-term upward pressure on aluminum prices; copper and nickel are not mentioned.

Trump issued proclamations expanding Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum imports into the US. For aluminum, the duty rate rises from 10% to 25%, exemptions and product exclusions are ended, and more “derivative” aluminum products are added within a defined mechanism effective March 12. This should support higher aluminum prices short term. | The article outlines February 10, 2025 US proclamations ending steel/aluminum tariff exemptions and exclusions and imposing across-the-board duties, including raising aluminum tariffs from 10% to 25% for most sources and extending 25% duties to additional “derivative” aluminum products from March 12, 2025. | Ontario Rivers Alliance raised concerns with Canada Nickel’s Crawford Project review, citing a lack of detail on cumulative impacts, tailings “in-process” carbon capture claims, and water/effluent management. The objections could increase regulatory uncertainty and potential project delays or added costs, which may pressure near-term nickel sentiment.

Anglo American said it will sell its nickel business to MMG for up to $500 million, including $350 million at completion and potential earnouts/cash tied to project development. The divested assets produce about 38,000 tonnes of nickel annually. The restructuring targets a focus on copper and iron ore. | Anglo American agreed to sell its nickel business to MMG, covering two ferronickel operations in Brazil and related growth projects, for up to $500 million (including an initial $350 million on completion and contingent payments). Management framed it as portfolio simplification, implying limited near-term effect on nickel output. | Anglo American agreed to sell its nickel business to MMG for $350 million on completion, plus a $100 million price-linked earnout and $50 million contingent payment tied to final investment decisions. The assets include ferronickel operations in Brazil and greenfield projects, as part of a portfolio simplification. | Anglo American said it will sell its nickel business to China’s MMG for up to US$500 million. The transaction could shift long-term nickel supply ownership and production strategy, but it is not an immediate disruption or demand shock, so near-term nickel price impact is likely limited. | Anglo American said it will sell its nickel business to MMG for up to $500m as part of restructuring away from nickel and toward copper and iron ore. The divested assets produce about 38,000 t/year of nickel. The company expects cash at completion plus earnouts tied to development. | Anglo American agreed to sell its nickel division to MMG Singapore Resources for up to $500m, including $350m upfront and price-linked payments. The deal follows nickel selling near a four-year low, blamed on Indonesia/China supply oversupply, a stronger US dollar, and softer electric-vehicle demand. | Indonesia Battery Corporation (IBC) plans to build a nickel-based EV battery recycling plant by 2031, targeting recycling over 99% of components. The company says recycled nickel can be reused and should improve nickel availability and sustainability. While relevant to nickel, the long timeline suggests minimal near-term price impact. | Anglo American will sell its nickel business to MMG for up to $500 million, including Brazil ferronickel and development projects producing about 38,000 tonnes of nickel per year. The deal supports Anglo’s shift toward copper and iron ore, but the transfer is unlikely to immediately change physical nickel supply. | Indonesia relies heavily on Philippine nickel ore (about 97% of its imports). A proposed Philippines raw nickel export ban aimed at boosting domestic processing would threaten feedstock supply for Indonesian smelters. Experts urge diplomacy to secure alternative sources, but logistics and trade challenges could destabilize nickel supply and weigh on prices. | Anglo American agreed to sell its Brazilian nickel operations (Barro Alto, Codemin, and greenfield projects) to MMG for up to $500mn, with expected closing by September. The deal includes cash, earnout, and development contingents. It signals portfolio shifts amid weaker Brazilian ferronickel imports to China. | Anglo American said it has agreed to sell its nickel business to MMG Singapore Resources for up to $500 million, covering two ferronickel operations in Brazil. The deal follows other divestments and is framed as cash generation, with no immediate evidence of reduced nickel output.

First Atlantic Nickel reports a 400-meter step-out drill hole (402 meters) intersecting continuous, disseminated visible awaruite nickel-iron alloy at its RPM Zone in Newfoundland, expanding the footprint and leaving mineralization open. The company also states Phase 2 is fully funded and permitted, targeting further step-outs. | First Atlantic Nickel reports that RPM Hole 4 intersected 402 meters of continuous visible awaruite nickel-iron alloy mineralization, extending the RPM Zone to at least 400 meters long and 500 meters wide, with mineralization remaining open. Phase 2 drilling is fully funded and permitted, supporting further expansion. | Alliance Nickel’s NiWest project in Western Australia has published a definitive feasibility study and claims low-cost, low-carbon nickel sulphate production with a ~10% premium over LME nickel. Despite a depressed nickel market tied to Indonesian supply, FID is not until ~Aug 2025, limiting near-term price impact. | Anglo American agreed to sell its Nickel Brazil ferronickel business (Barro Alto and Codemin) to MMG for up to $500m, with part of the consideration linked to future nickel prices. Operations are expected to transfer by Q3 2025 subject to approvals, implying limited short-term supply impact. | The Philippines is considering a ban on exporting raw nickel ore to build domestic processing capacity, potentially starting after a five-year transition. Experts say this would concern Indonesia’s smelters, as most Indonesian nickel ore imports come from the Philippines, potentially tightening supply and raising international nickel prices and processing input costs. | Eramet announced leadership changes, with Christel Bories stepping down as CEO while remaining chair, and Paulo Castellari taking over. The focus includes Eramet’s SLN nickel mine/smelter in New Caledonia, where the group stated it is no longer financing SLN. Prior remarks questioned SLN’s competitiveness versus Indonesia. | Utah family businesses say a 25% tariff on imported steel and aluminum is already prompting supplier price increases and may be passed through to their costs. A steel container maker highlights large steel input expenses, while a brewery buys many aluminum cans annually and expects higher beer pricing and tighter margins.

VR Resources reports completion of its first winter drill program at the Silverback copper-gold project, intersecting shearing, alteration, veining and sulfide-bearing textures, and planning follow-up once assays arrive. The geological model also references nickel mineralization in mafic-ultramafic dykes, but no copper/nickel results or supply changes are provided. | The U.S. reinstated 25% tariffs on global steel imports and raised aluminum import duties from 10% to 25% effective March 12, ending prior exemptions and product exclusions. The tariffs apply to derivative products and can add duties atop existing AD/CV charges, likely weighing on aluminum-related demand expectations near term.

Trump issued Section 232 steel and aluminum tariff proclamations effective March 12, 2025, revising duty rates and limiting relief/exclusions for covered derivative products. The measures are aimed at imported aluminum articles and can reduce import inflows and increase landed costs in the U.S., potentially supporting aluminum prices over the next weeks. | President Trump issued Section 232 proclamations reimposing blanket additional tariffs on imported aluminum (and derivative aluminum) into the US, with effects slated for March 12, 2025. The measures rescind prior exemptions/quotas and limit Commerce’s ability to grant relief, increasing uncertainty and import costs for aluminum supply. | SMM reports Indonesian nickel ore domestic trade premiums rising, with supply disruptions from rainy season, Ramadan timing, and SIMBARA enforcement tightening compliant ore flows. Active procurement after incomplete inventories is lifting premiums. Higher ore costs support high-grade NPI prices and narrow smelter losses, while refined nickel supply ramps continue. | High-grade nickel pig iron prices rose sharply amid supply constraints: nickel pig iron smelters operate at losses and some Indonesian lines run at low capacity. Demand sentiment for stainless steel is improving, traders are buying as the NPI discount versus electrolytic nickel narrows, and macro signals support pure nickel, though downstream demand remains soft.

The piece highlights ACG Metals’ Gediktepe shift from oxide processing to sulphide ore production, targeting 20,000–25,000 tonnes of copper annually from 2026. It cites secured funding, fixed-price plant construction, and valuation upside tied to copper output, factors that could improve near-term copper sentiment.

Merdeka Battery Materials (MBMA) will build a $1.8 billion HPAL nickel processing plant at Indonesia’s Morowali Industrial Park. The project, operated by PT Sulawesi Nickel Cobalt, targets 90,000 tonnes of MHP capacity and aims to start by mid-2026, reinforcing battery-grade nickel supply plans. | U.S. proclamations reinstate 25% steel tariffs and raise aluminum tariffs to 25% from March 12, 2025, eliminating prior exemptions and tightening enforcement. The policy aims to protect domestic producers but is expected to raise input costs for consumers and trigger retaliation. Near term, aluminum faces demand and trade-tension risks. | Doubleview Gold Corp. announced high-grade copper (up to 1.00% over 107m) and gold intercepts from drilling at the Hat polymetallic deposit. The company says results suggest proximity to a porphyry feeder zone and extends mineralization. It is exploration news with no immediate production impact on metals markets.

Reuters reports Anglo American selling its Brazilian nickel mines and plants to China’s MMG. The deal is framed against heavy nickel oversupply from Indonesia and rising LME inventories, with Class II ferronickel holding cash-flow despite LME price lows. Chinese control of refining and product shifts are highlighted. | PT Merdeka Battery Materials said it has signed agreements to build a $1.8 billion high-pressure acid leach (HPAL) plant in Indonesia. The project targets 90,000 tonnes per year of nickel materials for EV batteries, with construction starting in January and commissioning expected in about 18 months. | First Atlantic Nickel reports initial drill-hole assays from the Super Gulp Zone at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland, finding consistent nickel mineralization across ~294 meters (up to 0.32% Ni) and ongoing DTR metallurgical testing for magnetic separation. The discovery expands a known nickel trend and supports future development expectations. | VR Resources reports maiden reconnaissance drilling at its Westwood Target (Empire Project) confirming a fertile mafic-ultramafic intrusion with sulfide segregation. Reported results include up to 0.09% Cu and 0.11% Ni at depth, alongside anomalous PGE. The findings may modestly boost expectations for future supply. | Merdeka Battery Materials, via an indirect stake in Sulawesi Nickel Cobalt, secured $1.4bn of debt financing for a new Indonesia HPAL plant on Sulawesi. The project targets 90,000 tpa of contained nickel in MHP, funded by domestic banks and supported by long-term laterite ore supply, with commissioning expected within 18 months. | The Argus Nickel Indonesia Conference highlights Indonesia’s large role in global nickel supply and forecasts growth in battery-grade nickel and nickel pig iron production. It focuses on networking and long-term commercial dealmaking across the nickel value chain. The event implies future supply/demand trends, but provides no concrete immediate market catalysts.

Indonesia’s regulation requires mining exporters to deposit 100% of foreign-exchange earnings into special accounts for 12 months (with partial domestic retention). While nickel products’ exports aren’t directly restricted, the article says FX constraints can increase costs, delay smelting investment, and weaken sentiment, pressuring nickel supply expectations. | Anglo American agreed to sell its nickel business to MMG Singapore, including the Barro Alto and Codemin ferronickel operations and related greenfield projects, with upfront cash plus price-linked earn-out tied to value and investment decisions. The deal may slightly tighten perceived nickel supply, supporting sentiment amid an oversupplied market. | STLLR Gold Inc. announced a proposed sale of its non-core Loveland Nickel Property to Loyalist Exploration for upfront cash, Loyalist shares, and a net smelter royalty. The transaction is part of streamlining toward its gold projects, implying limited immediate effect on nickel supply expectations.

High-grade nickel pig iron prices rose this week amid constrained NPI supply: smelters ran at losses with low production and Indonesia faced insufficient nickel ore feedstock. Demand improved as stainless steel mills resumed after Chinese New Year. Nickel pig iron strength supported tighter NPI vs. electrolytic nickel spreads, keeping short-term prices supported. | SMM says Indonesian laterite nickel ore premiums continue rising (though growth slows), with smelters still short of raw materials and expecting greater shipments after Sulawesi rains. Demand supports via stainless recovery and profit rebound. Indonesian DHE export FX rules may add short-term volatility. High-grade NPI hits new highs and costs ease. | Magna Mining completed its acquisition of KGHM affiliate assets in Ontario’s Sudbury Basin, including the producing McCreedy West copper mine and additional past-producing copper/nickel projects. The company will own and operate the copper mine following closing, potentially supporting near-term copper supply expectations versus demand.

FNArena’s broker ratings roundup includes Chalice Mining commentary on a Gonneville metallurgical breakthrough. Morgans says saleable copper and nickel concentrates can now be produced from low-grade composites, removing a complex hydromet process and reducing development risk and expected costs. The target was cut, but the project outlook improved. | Indonesia’s ESDM issued decrees revising the HMA/HPM benchmark-price calculation method for metallic minerals, including nickel ore, NPI and MHP, effective March 1, 2025. The first-period March reference nickel HMA and resulting HPM nickel-ore prices for various grades were published, with potential second-period prices later in March. | Kingsrose completed a 100% acquisition of the Jakon nickel-copper-cobalt project in Finland from Rio Tinto, expanding its position in the Kotalahti belt. The project is near the former Hitura nickel-copper mine and has historical nickel-copper sulphide intercepts. Payments are mostly deferred and contingent on a future FID. | Anglo American sold its Brazilian nickel operations and developing projects for $500m, citing a highly competitive market dominated by Indonesia’s growing ferronickel output. The piece notes nickel prices have fallen for two years due to oversupply. Despite the transaction, it signals portfolio change more than immediate market supply disruption. | Doug Ford threatened to stop nickel shipments to the U.S. on the eve of a trade war. The move implies potential disruption of cross-border nickel supply flows into the U.S., increasing trade risk and uncertainty for near-term nickel availability and pricing.

The report says Indonesia’s “nickel boom” has pushed conditions such that local nickel smelters are being forced to shut down. This implies lower near-term nickel refining volumes and potentially tighter market supply, which typically supports nickel prices in the following days to weeks. | Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Ontario will stop nickel shipments to the U.S. if the U.S. imposes proposed tariffs on Canadian goods. He also mentioned stockpiling nickel and potential additional retaliation measures. The threatened disruption is directly linked to near-term nickel supply flows to the U.S. | Ontario Premier Doug Ford said the province would stop nickel shipments into the U.S. if U.S. tariffs on Canadian goods take effect, adding nickel stockpiling and retaliatory procurement legislation. Ivanhoe Mines updated production at the DRC’s Kamoa-Kakula copper complex and Kipushi zinc mine. Australia’s market drops broadly amid negative global cues. CNBC discusses whether Trump’s reciprocal tariffs could trigger a commodities “super-cycle.” It cites higher copper front-month prices but warns tariffs may raise slowdown risks that pressure copper short term. It says nickel is unlikely to surge, while aluminium is among materials expected to be repriced upward as trade tensions persist. | Ardea Resources completed 287 infill reverse circulation drill holes at its Goongarrie Hub nickel project in Western Australia, reporting multiple high-grade nickel intercepts at Big Four and Scotia Dam. Management says results support potential resource upgrades and pit optimization work in its ongoing definitive feasibility study, which can strengthen near-term nickel sentiment. | The piece outlines U.S. reinstatement of full tariffs on steel (25%) and aluminum (25%) with a higher China rate and a 90-day pause that excludes steel and aluminum. It emphasizes continued elevated tariff burdens until at least July 9, 2025, likely weighing on aluminum-linked demand and prices near term.

Clean Energy Transition Inc. filed an NI 43-101 Technical Report supporting an updated Mineral Resource Estimate for its Aurora Nickel Project in Ontario. The Indicated Resource totals 10.06 Mt at 0.42% nickel, with open-pit and underground deposits. The company frames the update as strengthening the low-carbon nickel opportunity. | Talon Metals said drill hole 25TK0561 at its Tamarack nickel-copper project intersected 16.09 meters of mixed massive sulphide with 10%–80% sulphide content, potentially extending mineralization at depth. Management links the result to earlier BHEM modeling success and suggests potential tonnage addition as feasibility work progresses. | Talon Metals announced a mixed massive sulphide intercept (16.09m) at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project, with sulphide content indicating higher nickel grades. The company frames results as extending the CGO East Zone below the current resource and supporting potential early tonnage, but it remains feasibility-stage exploration news. | Kincora Copper announced an expanded earn-in/JV with AngloGold Ashanti to broaden drilling across the Nyngan, Nevertire and Nevertire South projects in NSW, targeting copper-gold porphyry systems. The update notes ongoing scout drilling and prior anomalous copper results, but remains exploration-focused with no production or logistics changes. | Bloomberg reports a production halt and investigation around Indonesia’s Morowali nickel hub after a landslide at a tailings area tied to PT QMB New Energy. Fatalities occurred, traders expect reduced output, and nearby producers cut production. Indonesia’s large share of global nickel supply raises immediate bullish pricing pressure.

The piece describes repeated deadly incidents and alleged systematic safety shortcomings at Tsingshan’s IMIP nickel processing and stainless steel facilities in Indonesia, including a December 2023 nickel-plant explosion and another fatal smelter accident in February 2025. Despite production resuming quickly after one death, the accidents heighten perceived supply-risk and oversight concerns.

Indonesia’s nickel smelters association asked the government to delay higher mineral royalty fees until LME nickel prices reach $17,000/ton, above current levels (~$15,600). The new royalties (14%–19% on ore; higher rates on nickel pig iron and matte) would raise costs amid weakening nickel prices. | Indonesia plans higher nickel royalties, replacing a flat 10% levy on nickel ore with a variable rate of 14%-19% linked to government-set price levels. Lower-grade ore for battery-grade nickel faces a reduced 2% royalty. The change takes effect shortly, while miners warn of margin strain and output cuts. | Realtor.com reports that Trump’s 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports could raise construction costs for newly built residences. Builders may pass higher costs to consumers or delay finishing construction, reducing housing supply. This could push rents higher in multiple US metros as tariffs dampen development plans.

Indonesia dominates global nickel output and is pushing downstream processing after a raw ore export ban, building smelters and HPAL capacity. Nickel’s role in EV cathodes underpins demand growth from automakers. However, expanded refining could also increase refined supply, making the net near-term price effect uncertain. | Copper rose to a two-week high as a weaker U.S. dollar encouraged CTA/fund buying and traders covered shorts, breaching key moving averages. However, sentiment remains cautious because Trump’s import tariffs threaten growth and industrial metal demand. Aluminium and nickel were also up, but driven largely by macro flows. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. said Phase 1 construction is complete on an all-season restricted access road to its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project, replacing floatplane/ATV logistics with year-round road access. The company is starting Phase 2 and plans further upgrades and 2025 next steps to expand resources, potentially improving future supply prospects. | Glenstar Minerals announced it has hired Midnight Sun Drilling to conduct the first-ever drill program at its Green Monster nickel-copper-cobalt property in Nevada. The company plans to define the extent of mineralization after permitting and expects updates soon, citing earlier sampling with meaningful Cu and Ni values. | Glenstar Minerals hired Midnight Sun Drilling to run the first-ever drill program on its Green Monster nickel-copper-cobalt property in Nevada. The project is undrilled to date and targets shallow RC drilling based on prior surface sampling that included copper and nickel grades. Results are expected in coming days. | Alaska Energy Metals filed an updated NI 43-101 technical report for its Eureka deposit at the Nikolai nickel project in Alaska, raising tonnage, metal content, and nickel-equivalent grades versus the prior 2024 estimate. The company links the update to U.S. critical-mineral supply-chain efforts, but no near-term production changes are stated.

Talon Metals said feasibility study drilling at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project found massive sulphide mineralization extending laterally east of the prior southern resource boundary, including a 1.9m intercept logged at ~70% sulphides. The release continues drilling in Minnesota and references planned rigs in Michigan.

Vedanta is exploring a US IPO for its 80% owned Konkola Copper Mines in Zambia to raise about $1bn for mine development. The plan follows regained control after provisional liquidation and financing to restart operations, with production targets rising. More funding prospects can lift short-term copper supply expectations.

SMM’s weekly review finds Indonesian nickel ore premiums stable-to-slightly higher for pyrometallurgy, but hydrometallurgical ore prices may soften after a hydromet project accident. High-grade NPI FOB prices fell WoW, with sluggish downstream stainless demand and deeper smelter losses. Nickel ore supply stays tight, but NPI remains pressured. | High-nickel pig iron (8–12% NPI) prices fell further this week in China and Indonesia, with smelters showing low raw-material buying due to shrinking profits. Weak stainless steel demand, influenced by reciprocal tariffs, is expected to keep pressure on NPI. The discount to electrolytic nickel widened, signaling softer nickel demand.

Beijing-driven LFP dominance in Indonesia is eroding demand for nickel-based EV batteries. The article notes that about 90% of last year’s 43,000 EVs used nickel-free LFP chemistries, largely from Chinese automakers, leading LG Energy Solution to withdraw from a major Indonesia battery investment, signaling weaker nickel demand prospects. | The piece highlights a rebound case for commodities tied to infrastructure spending and electrification, claiming electrification will require sharply higher copper production and that materials demand will rise with supply-chain realignment. It also mentions nickel as part of electrification-related minerals and suggests pro-growth policies will support commodity prices. | Nickel 28 reported Q1 2025 Ramu operations: contained nickel in MHP fell to 6,970 tonnes and nickel sales to 6,133 tonnes, driven by production curtailment from an acid plant blower fan failure. Unit costs rose to $3.61/lb. It notes lower Q1 LME average nickel price at $7.06/lb.

Indonesia implemented new non-tax royalty (PNBP) rates for nickel products effective 26 April. Rates vary by HMA-based nickel reference price for ore and are set for NPI, ferronickel, matte, and newly introduced MHP. The change is expected to raise longer-term costs but likely has limited immediate price impact.

Power Metallic reported drill results from its Lion Zone at the Nisk polymetallic project, including copper-dominant massive sulphides and assays containing nickel. While the high-grade results may improve investor sentiment for future Cu/Ni resource potential, the article does not imply immediate production changes, so near-term price impact is likely limited. | DW reports leaked internal documents alleging systematic chromium-6 contamination from Harita Group’s Obi Island nickel mining and smelting complex, including claims made during the 2023 IPO due diligence. Residents lack clean water. IRMA is reviewing the site for certification, and legal scrutiny may follow, but no immediate supply cut is stated. | High-nickel pig iron prices in China fell again, though the weekly decline slowed, amid continued weak finished-product demand and cautious stainless steel buyers. Pure nickel also faced pressure from tariff/macro uncertainty and a surplus supply-demand balance, with downstream nickel plate transactions weak. Cost support limits downside somewhat. | First Atlantic Nickel says early drilling at Newfoundland ophiolite projects returned strong results, including a new “RPM zone” discovery, with metallurgical recovery testing underway. The company highlights processing awaruite nickel without smelting (lower energy, no sulfur removal). It also explores geologic hydrogen tied to the deposits, funded by researchers and U.S. support. | Glencore reported first-quarter copper output down 30% to 167,900 tonnes, despite keeping full-year 2025 production guidance unchanged. Nickel production fell 21% as well. The company cited tariff-driven uncertainty and potential trade-flow dislocation, while analysts reacted to the weaker quarterly physical supply.

Tartisan Nickel Corp. says it has started a 4-week airborne geophysical survey and a greenfields exploration program at its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project in northwestern Ontario. The work aims to refine subsurface targets for future prospecting, sampling, and drilling. No production or supply change is announced.

NewRange Copper Nickel announced the NorthMet project was designated a “Transparency Project” by a federal council aimed at expanding domestic critical-mineral supply. The move signals prioritized permitting progress for a major undeveloped copper and nickel resource. The company reports most state permits secured and plans to update Section 404 filings. | NewRange Copper Nickel said the federal NEDC designated its NorthMet project as a “Transparency Project,” prioritizing permitting for critical minerals. The firm holds most required permits and plans to update its Clean Water Act Section 404 application. The designation is intended to speed development of domestic copper and nickel supply with environmental safeguards.

Vale says it is evaluating strategic options for some nickel assets, including potential divestment or placing operations in care and maintenance, due to low nickel prices and short-term oversupply from Indonesia. The company is also exploring partnerships for Sudbury facilities to cut costs and maintain competitiveness.

Ivanhoe Mines said crews have entered the Platreef Flatreef orebody underground for the first time, with the first blast on May 7, 2025. The polymetallic deposit includes high-grade nickel and copper, and ore is being prepared for Phase 1 concentrator feed in Q4 2025. | Ivanhoe Mines said crews have entered the high-grade Flatreef orebody for the first time and completed initial underground development, including the first blast of ore on May 7, 2025. The polymetallic deposit includes nickel and copper as payable by-products, with stored ore ahead of Phase 1 concentrator feed in Q4 2025. | Nickel Asia Corp.’s Q1 profit rose sharply, supported by higher saprolite and limonite ore prices. Realized nickel ore sales prices increased year on year, and limonite pricing is linked to the London Metal Exchange. The company also sold part of its CBNC stake, while March deliveries were impacted by HPAL maintenance. | Green Bridge Metals reports a non-binding LOI to option the Serpentine copper-nickel sulphide project in Minnesota, with planned future option funding and continued drilling/permitting progress. The company highlights strong historic recoveries and infrastructure access. However, the LOI is not definitive and implies no immediate production impact for copper or nickel. | Green Bridge Metals entered a non-binding LOI to option the Serpentine copper-nickel sulfide project in Minnesota. The release links the plan to a U.S. executive order aimed at boosting American mineral production and cites permitting progress. It supports bullish sentiment for copper and nickel, though impact is likely limited near-term.

Canada Nickel closed a US$20 million bridge loan to fund the Crawford nickel sulphide project and repay part of its Auramet loan, plus a corporate update on acquiring additional Timmins-area claims. The financing reduces near-term project funding risk, which is generally supportive for expectations around nickel supply.

The US partially reversed “Liberation Day” tariffs on selected critical minerals, but Indonesia signals it could use critical minerals as leverage, citing its large nickel reserves (34% of global). The risk of non-tariff restrictions/export limits is highlighted, which would likely tighten near-term nickel supply expectations. | Green Bridge Metals announces the acquisition of the Copper–Nickel–PGE Serpentine project in Minnesota, USA. The deal is a mining-stage development move that may influence longer-term supply expectations for copper and nickel, but the article provides no near-term operational changes that would likely drive spot price shifts. | Green Bridge Metals says it has acquired the Copper-Nickel-PGE serpentine project in Minnesota. The deal links the company to copper and nickel resources, potentially shaping longer-term supply expectations, but there is no immediate production, permitting, or shipping change cited that would likely move near-term copper or nickel prices.

Glenstar Minerals says it will begin a 10–14 day reverse-circulation drilling program at its Green Monster Ni-Cu-Co property in Nevada. The target is within a district known for copper and also nickel anomalies. Sampling has shown high Cu and Ni grades, but the release contains no new assay results. | Glenstar Minerals says drilling will start May 14 on its Green Monster Ni-Cu-Co property in Nevada, using a reverse-circulation rig over 10–14 days with five drill holes. Prior sampling reported elevated copper and nickel values, which may modestly support sentiment for copper and nickel.

The piece says the US partially lifted tariffs on some critical minerals, while Indonesia (with 34% of global nickel reserves) may use nickel as bargaining leverage. It discusses possible policy concessions versus a continued ore export ban, implying near-term supply and market-access uncertainty for nickel. | Nickel’s decline is cited as the strongest driver behind falling 304 stainless steel surcharges, pulling down the Stainless Monthly Metals Index and pressuring service-center selling prices. Mills kept discounts high and struggled to raise prices as Asia remained the price floor and nickel-linked surcharge levels continued to fall.

Eurobattery Minerals’ subsidiary Finncobalt signed a non-binding letter of intent with Terrafame to refine nickel (and cobalt) from its Hautalampi project, with all production processed at Terrafame’s facilities. Terrafame owns Europe’s largest nickel mine. The framework may support expected nickel demand/offtake, though not binding. | ShFE is considering opening its domestic nickel futures contract to foreign investors under China’s QFII programme this year, rather than creating a separate INE contract. The plan aims to boost global accessibility and provide an alternative to LME nickel after the 2022 trading suspension, pending CSRC approval.

Reuters reports Europe’s large energy storage buildout is using lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries, accelerating a shift away from nickel-containing chemistries. CRU data show nickel battery-use intensity has fallen sharply, and analysts expect LFP dominance to further weigh on nickel prices, with oversupply already depressing benchmarks. | The IEA warns that critical-mineral supply chains are fragile due to export restrictions and geopolitical concentration, with copper facing particular stress as demand for electricity-network expansion rises. It projects worsening fundamentals for copper and highlights supplier concentration for nickel (notably Indonesia), supporting expectations of higher prices.

North Maluku police arrested 11 residents after protests against a nickel mining company in East Halmahera. Authorities said protesters carried sharp weapons, seized keys to mining vehicles, and obstructed legal operations. Protesters alleged environmental damage and forest clearing. Charges include threats, extortion, and obstruction, potentially disrupting nickel mining activity.

Indonesia’s nickel miners association says the industry faces major hurdles replacing coal with alternative energy sources despite global pressure for greener operations. The shift to new power plants is difficult in ore-rich regions lacking clean energy infrastructure. With growing processing capacity, uncertainty over coal transition may constrain nickel output or raise costs. | The European Commission’s steel & metals action plan is discussed through a nickel-industry lens. The article warns that overly stringent environmental rules (EQS/REACH), energy price disadvantages, and policy design could undermine nickel project permitting and competitiveness, while supporting state aid and circularity. Net near-term price impact is uncertain.

Indonesia’s Danantara Indonesia, INA, and Eramet signed an MoU to explore an investment platform for nickel, targeting an integrated EV battery raw-materials ecosystem from upstream to downstream. The parties will conduct preliminary assessments and prepare a collaboration roadmap, with Eramet contributing technical expertise and financing structures. | Lundin Mining and BHP’s Vicuña joint venture announced a major greenfield discovery at the Filo del Sol project in Argentina, estimating at least 13 million tons of copper plus sizable gold and silver resources, with expansion potential. While environmental concerns exist, the headline primarily supports copper supply expectations. | Glenstar Minerals reports completion of an initial reverse-circulation drill program on its Green Monster Ni-Cu-Cobalt property in Nevada under budget. Drilling found oxide and sulfide mineralization and dolomite veining hosting fine-grained sulfides across targets. Samples are being shipped for assay, with no new results released yet. | Glenstar Minerals reports completion of its initial reverse-circulation drill program on the Green Monster property in Nevada. Drilling intersected oxide and sulfide mineralized sequences and dolomite veining with local sulfide mineralization, targeting nickel-copper and anomalous cobalt. Assay results from these new samples were not yet provided. | Rumors that Indonesia may ease RKAB (mine quota) approvals have made nickel market sentiment volatile. Even so, ESDM officials reported no major supplemental quota approvals in two weeks, and Indonesian nickel ore prices remained firm. Weather disruptions and weak NPI economics cap upside for fire-grade ore, while hydromet demand improved after April outages. | The headline says a North Dakota coal mine will become a processing site for Minnesota nickel. That suggests changes to nickel processing operations that could affect near-term supply and refining economics. The article text is largely missing, so the magnitude and timing of any nickel price impact are uncertain.

A retired North Dakota coal mine is being repurposed into a nickel processing plant. The development suggests upcoming additional nickel processing capacity, which could gradually improve supply availability and weigh on nickel price expectations over the coming weeks, depending on commissioning timeline and throughput. | North Dakota’s Beulah will host a processing facility operated by Talon Metals to process Minnesota-mined nickel ore, shipped by rail to the site. Tesla is contracted to buy roughly half the processed nickel, with Defense Department use also expected. The project supports U.S. critical-mineral sourcing away from China. | Eramet and Indonesia’s Danantara/Indonesia Investment Authority signed an MoU to explore investments across the nickel supply chain, from upstream mining to downstream processing for EV battery materials. The deal, framed as part of a broader France-Indonesia economic push, centers on Indonesia’s large nickel reserves and production potential. | Lifezone Metals announced filing dates for technical report summaries for the Kabanga Nickel Project in Tanzania, including an Initial Assessment filing before market open June 2 and a Feasibility Study in July. The studies cover staged development and hydrometallurgical refinery plans, including the project’s first mineral reserves disclosure. | Talon Metals, owner of a taconite mine in Minnesota, plans to build the Beulah Minerals Processing Facility in North Dakota with Westmoreland Coal. The plant is designed to process nickel and copper minerals, using local energy and fly ash byproducts. Start is planned for 2027, with operations by 2029. | Talon Metals plans to build the Beulah Minerals Processing Facility on a former coal site in North Dakota, partnering with Westmoreland Coal. The project is described as advanced processing for nickel and copper minerals, using energy, rail transport, and coal fly ash. Construction is targeted for 2027, startup 2029.

Brazil’s ONS denied Vale’s request to increase power supply to its northern Onca Puma nickel complex after Vale missed a deadline to contract additional electricity. The second furnace for the $555 million expansion is still targeted for the second half of 2025, but approval uncertainty raises near-term nickel supply/ramp risks.

Danantara Indonesia, INA, and Eramet signed an MoU in Jakarta to explore an integrated nickel investment platform in Indonesia, spanning upstream mining to downstream EV battery raw materials. The partners will assess suitable projects and roadmap financing, aiming to strengthen Indonesia’s nickel supply chain for energy-transition demand. | First Atlantic Nickel says it will attend a Benchmark Mineral Intelligence conference and promote its Atlantic Nickel awaruite project, claiming low-energy, smelter-free processing and improved compliance with U.S. IRA FEOC rules. It also reports ongoing Phase 2 drilling and acquisition of additional licenses. The news is supportive but not production-impacting. | Lifezone Metals said it has released an initial assessment for its Kabanga nickel project in northwest Tanzania. The company expects to complete a feasibility-study technical report summary in July 2025. Results are preliminary and include resources not yet converted to reserves, with no certainty the economics will materialize. | Vale’s nickel output expansion at Brazil’s Onca Puma complex hit a hurdle after power-grid operator ONS declined Vale’s request to raise electricity consumption needed for a new furnace. Missing contract deadlines left power allocated elsewhere, potentially delaying added capacity of 15,200 tonnes annually and pressuring near-term nickel supply expectations. | Indonesia launched its first national ESG forum for the nickel industry, bringing together miners, policymakers and investors to develop locally tailored sustainability standards. With the EU tightening rules, the initiative aims to secure responsible supply chains for battery and clean-energy demand. Government backing suggests ESG integration into policy soon. | First Atlantic Nickel announces participation in Benchmark Mineral Intelligence’s GIGA USA conference, highlighting its Atlantic Nickel Project where awaruite (~75% nickel) can be concentrated via magnetic separation and flotation without overseas smelting. The company argues this supports North American nickel supply under U.S. IRA FEOC requirements, boosting nickel sentiment. | Trump’s planned policy doubles tariffs on imported steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% effective Wednesday. The article says higher costs for U.S. manufacturers and can/bottle users will be passed through, raising prices in sectors like autos, appliances, and canned foods. Aluminium demand-cost effects are most direct.

Benton Resources reported an early drill intercept of gold at its Great Burnt Copper-Gold project and noted visible chalcopyrite, with forthcoming base-metal assays. It also describes further drilling and EM surveys that expand targeting, with drilling paused for results. No production changes are announced; nickel is only referenced via a JV interest. | The article says Trump’s plan to double tariffs on steel and aluminum to 50% could lift grocery prices by increasing costs for can and packaging materials, including imported tinplate steel used for food cans. It also notes potential substitution from aluminum to plastic and higher logistics costs, pressuring retailer margins and consumer prices. | Talon Metals announced new developments for its Tamarack nickel-copper project in North Dakota, including an agreement giving it rights to buy the Beulah Mine for processing and recent higher-grade intercept results. The company updated its “mine in a box” design to address water and dust concerns and aims to begin the EIS process soon.

Trump’s executive order has put a 50% tariff on foreign steel and aluminum imports into effect in the US, with most partners except the UK. The move is aimed at countering low-priced, excess imports, notably from China, and is expected to burden exporters like Canada and Mexico immediately. | AFP reports that Indonesia’s Weda Bay nickel concession is encroaching on Indigenous forest lands, triggering protests, arrests, and increasing calls for protection measures. With Eramet seeking review of engagement protocols and government acknowledging isolated tribes, risks of operational or expansion delays could weigh on nickel sentiment. | Trump’s administration raised US steel and aluminium import tariffs, doubling them to as much as 50% effective Wednesday (UK imports exempted at 25% due to a trade deal). The move is expected to raise costs for US manufacturers and consumers and may support aluminium price expectations near term, with potential retaliatory risk. | Trump will raise US tariffs on nearly all imported steel and aluminium to 50% from Wednesday, doubling the current 25% rate (except for the UK). The policy is meant to protect domestic industry and counter excess foreign supply, likely lifting aluminium input costs and prices in the near term. | The article says US tariffs on steel and aluminum imports doubled to 50% and are likely to push up costs for construction, vehicles, appliances and cans. Aluminum prices had risen earlier, and the Aluminum Association warns higher tariffs could reduce Canadian raw-aluminum supply to US refiners, tightening availability and supporting higher aluminum prices. | Trump doubled US tariffs on imported steel and aluminum to 50% from 25%, citing national security and oversupply. The change threatens higher input costs for US manufacturers and drew retaliation threats from Canada and Mexico. The piece notes aluminum smelting’s electricity cost and Canada’s export impact, but no direct copper/nickel linkage. | The U.S. doubled import tariffs on steel and aluminum to 50% from 25%, citing national security and oversupply abroad. The move has triggered retaliation threats and cost concerns from automakers and homebuilders. For aluminum, higher import friction is likely to tighten U.S. supply and pressure prices upward over coming days. | Trump’s executive order doubles U.S. tariffs on imported steel and aluminum from 25% to 50%, effective immediately. Canadian officials warn of “mass disruption” and job losses, while negotiators express optimism. The OECD links tariffs to weaker growth, and producer prices for steel have risen, indicating near-term aluminum price pressure but timing is uncertain. | The U.S. will raise tariffs on most imported steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% beginning Wednesday, with a special 25% exception for U.K. metal. The move is intended to protect domestic producers and may raise costs for downstream manufacturers, likely supporting aluminium prices in the near term. | The U.S. plans to double most steel and aluminum import tariffs to 50%, effective after midnight Wednesday, while keeping a 25% rate for U.K. metal under a trade deal. Economists expect higher input costs across packaging, autos, and construction, with possible retaliation and demand pressure. | The U.S. increased tariffs on imported aluminium and steel from 25% to 50%, effective immediately, amid U.S.-EU trade talks. The move is already linked to aluminium market volatility and higher price premiums, with expectations that volumes may not fall quickly but prices could firm. Copper and nickel impacts are only indirect. | Trump’s proclamation will raise U.S. tariffs on nearly all imported steel and aluminum to 50% effective Wednesday, doubling from 25% for most sources. The move is intended to protect domestic producers and counter cheap excess imports, likely lifting landed aluminum costs and supporting higher aluminum prices. Copper and nickel are not directly discussed. | Trump hiked nearly all U.S. steel and aluminum import tariffs to 50%, ending the prior 25% rate (with the UK temporarily exempted at 25%). The move is expected to increase input costs for automakers, builders, and packaging, and likely support higher aluminum prices via reduced foreign supply and tighter market conditions. | Trump’s administration raised most US steel and aluminium import tariffs to 50% effective Wednesday, following prior increases to 25%. The move targets excess foreign supply and is expected to increase input costs for automakers and home builders. Analysts warn demand could soften, affecting metal prices, especially aluminium. | The article explains how a doubled U.S. tariff on steel and aluminum imports to 50% may raise costs for consumers across canned foods, cars, construction, and appliances. It notes high U.S. reliance on imports, potential supply tightening, and industry warnings that businesses will face higher input prices and uncertainty.

The U.S. doubled steel and aluminum tariffs to 50%, prompting calls in Canada for retaliation. Prime Minister Mark Carney says the tariffs are unjustified but is delaying reprisals while negotiations progress. Canadian industry argues aluminum demand will collapse and highlights job risks, even as a deal may be near. | Industry experts expect global nickel oversupply to persist as Indonesia’s production capacity expands and nickel demand growth slows. The article links weaker demand (including shift to LFP batteries) and compressed smelter margins to potential production cutbacks. Prices have halved over three years and remain pressured, reinforcing bearish sentiment. | Indonesia is investigating alleged nickel mining in Raja Ampat, West Papua. Greenpeace claims forests were cleared and sediments threaten coral reefs. Environment officials and the minerals minister plan reviews, identify the operator (Antam’s Gag Nikel), and say operations may be halted while documentation is checked. | Tsingshan reduced nickel pig iron output in Indonesia as profit margins shrank and the nickel market weakened. Indonesia’s NPI output is forecast to fall from 4.76mn tonnes in 2024 to 1.74mn this year, with further cuts possible if conditions persist, potentially tightening nickel supply. | Talon Metals reported record drill intercept assays at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project, including 34.9 meters at 28.88% NiEq and 57.76% CuEq. The results validate a BHEM-targeted zone and may improve long-run supply expectations, but no production timing is given. | Talon Metals reported record drill assays from drill hole 25TK0563 at the Tamarack nickel-copper project, including 34.9m grading 28.88% NiEq (and 57.76% CuEq). The company frames results as world-class and will guide further drilling, but there is no immediate production change. | President Trump raised Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum articles and derivative products from 25% to 50%, effective June 4, 2025 with strict customs declaration requirements. The change affects aluminum import competitiveness and can tighten near-term supply, supporting aluminum prices. The notice does not directly reference copper or nickel. | Trump’s 50% tariffs on imported steel and aluminum (double the prior rates) took effect June 4, raising protectionism concerns and inflation worries. Domestic producers praised the move, while Canada and the EU condemned it and promised countermeasures. Economists warn tariff-driven price hikes and demand pressure could follow. | Indonesia temporarily halted PT Gag Nickel’s operations in Raja Ampat due to allegations of environmental threats. The energy ministry will conduct field verification before deciding further actions, while the environment minister will review reports and may pursue legal action. The suspension creates immediate nickel supply risk. | Indonesia’s PT Ceria Nugraha Indotama plans to expand ferronickel capacity after starting production in late April. Its Rectangular Rotary Kiln Electric Furnace has initial annual capacity of 63,200 mt ferronickel. The company moves ahead despite expectations of nickel market oversupply in coming years, which can pressure prices. | BHP is weighing a return to nickel through a proposed $1 billion mine project in Tanzania, positioned as a lower-cost producer than Indonesia’s nickel industry. BHP exited nickel last year after suspending West Australian operations, and the backdrop includes Indonesia’s supply supported by Chinese backing.

Trump’s administration raised aluminum and steel import tariffs to 50%. A Mid-Michigan scrap firm expects tariffs to boost domestic production, tighten substitutes, and increase demand for recycled aluminum and steel scrap used in metalmaking. The CEO says recycled aluminum pricing is already strong and could rise if tariffs persist and are enforced. | Indonesia’s energy ministry has temporarily suspended Gag Nikel’s nickel mining on Gag Island in Raja Ampat while it verifies operations near a marine conservation area. The government says it is investigating potential violations with other agencies, creating a short-term nickel supply disruption risk for markets. | Indonesia’s environment ministry will review nickel mining in Raja Ampat after Greenpeace sparked public outcry over environmental damage. The energy ministry has suspended operations at a nickel mine pending inspection, with potential legal action and punishment for firms. Indonesia is a major nickel producer, so risks to output could support prices.

The piece warns that Indonesia’s expanding nickel mining in Raja Ampat—cited as necessary for the green transition—has cleared forests and coastal areas and threatens coral ecosystems. Multiple companies are under investigation for environmental permit violations and improper waste handling, raising the risk of enforcement or operational disruption and supply uncertainty for nickel. | Indonesia’s tighter domestic nickel ore supply has pushed up in-country nickel ore prices and premiums, with rainy weather disrupting Sulawesi operations and the government ordering Antam to suspend mining in a protected area. Ore buyers are seeking more imported supply. Downstream NPI and stainless prices fell, but upstream tightness is bullish for nickel. | Lawmakers and celebrities, backed by environmentalists, are urging Indonesia to permanently end nickel mining on Gag Island in Raja Ampat. The push follows outcry and Greenpeace Indonesia videos alleging environmental damage. Officials are calling for a moratorium or permanent ban and permit revocations, raising near-term nickel supply risk. | Indonesia’s energy minister ordered a temporary suspension of nickel mining in Raja Ampat, after inspections found serious environmental-law violations and protesters raised concerns about ecosystem damage. The halt applies pending field verification of PT Gag and relates to multiple mining sites on small islands, potentially tightening near-term nickel supply.

Indonesia revoked mining permits of four nickel mining firms in Raja Ampat, Papua, citing environmental regulation breaches and violations related to protected geopark areas. The minister said one company (PT GAG) remains unaffected due to its distance from the geopark and compliance planning, but others face permit loss. | Indonesia’s government revoked mining business licenses for four nickel operations in Raja Ampat (Southwest Papua) but allowed PT Gag Nikel to keep its permit. The move follows environmental concerns and regulatory violations, and comes before RKAB approvals are obtained for mining activities. | Indonesia suspended four nickel mining operations in Raja Ampat after an environmental investigation found permit violations. The companies had not started due to missing work-plan approval, while one remaining operator (Gag Nikel) continues outside the geopark. The move raises near-term concerns about nickel supply from Indonesia. | Copper prices fell as investors avoided the market amid uncertainty over U.S.-China trade talks and the trade war’s impact on demand. LME three-month copper was down 0.3%. Comex futures dropped too, while LME stocks continued to fall, and aluminium and nickel also eased. | Indonesia’s energy minister said the government revoked nickel ore mining permits for four firms in Raja Ampat, Papua, after environmental concerns triggered public protests. The decision risks cutting near-term nickel ore supply from a major producing country, which can support nickel prices over the next days to weeks.

A Jakarta Post report says nickel mining and processing at PT Indonesia Weda Bay Industrial Park (IWIP) near Gemaf village in North Maluku causes persistent health problems, with dust and ash blamed on the plant and its coal-fired power units. The social and regulatory risk could pressure nickel operations. | Indonesia’s energy minister denied claims that former President Joko Widodo and his wife were involved in nickel mining in Raja Ampat and said permits were not issued by the former administration. The episode followed viral allegations of nickel-ore shipping tied to vessel names resembling Jokowi’s initials. | Canada Nickel reported an initial resource estimate for its Mann West nickel sulphide project near Timmins, Ontario, including indicated and inferred nickel tonnes plus palladium/platinum resources. The company framed Mann West as a larger resource and part of multiple new nickel resource updates expected in 2025, boosting nickel supply expectations.

The Philippines dropped a planned ban on exporting unprocessed nickel ore, reversing a Senate-approved measure aimed at promoting domestic processing by 2030. Philippine ore exports rose in 2024, and dynamics in China/Indonesia are mixed, but the policy change supports continued ore supply and may weigh on nickel prices. | GT Resources provides exploration updates for its North Rock (Canada) and LK (Finland) copper–PGE–nickel projects, including BHEM survey results and discussion of historic and 2022 resource figures (notably large contained copper and nickel). The news is exploration-oriented with no immediate supply or demand change, limiting price impact.  | The Trump administration announced it will lift Biden-era restrictions on copper-nickel mining near Minnesota’s Boundary Waters, advancing the Twin Metals Minnesota project. The decision reverses a 20-year mineral withdrawal/moratorium upstream of the proposed mine site, improving prospects for additional domestic copper and nickel supply. | The Trump administration moved to lift Biden-era restrictions on copper-nickel mining near Minnesota’s Boundary Waters, reinstating/accelerating leases for the proposed Twin Metals Minnesota project. Supporters cite environmental safeguards and domestic critical-minerals supply; critics warn of acid drainage and plan legal challenges.

SMM’s weekly review reports a rise in Indonesian hydrometallurgical laterite nickel ore delivered prices (up ~3.8% WoW) amid weather-driven supply tightness and delayed quota approvals. Demand strengthened after Morowali resumed production, keeping hydrometallurgical ore prices firm. Meanwhile, NPI smelters face cost inversions as finished stainless prices fall.

India has sent geologists to Zambia to explore potential copper and cobalt deposits under a large government allocation. The initiative spans three years and aims to inform potential future mining leases. Because exploration is early and not affecting near-term supply, any impact on copper prices is likely limited. | CATL’s consortium with ANTAM and IBC broke ground on a $6 billion Indonesia project covering nickel mining, smelting, cathode materials, recycling, and battery manufacturing. Planned production includes about 142,000 tonnes of nickel annually. The integrated rollout should raise near-term expectations for nickel feedstock demand.

Indonesia’s nickel miners’ association APNI urged the government to keep mining quotas valid for three years rather than cutting RKABs to one year. The ministry said shorter quotas aim to better control supply and support prices amid revenue concerns. APNI warned shorter periods could cause approval bottlenecks for miners, raising supply uncertainty. | Mirasol reports a second drill hole at its Sobek (Vicuña, Chile) Potro SE target, extending a hydrothermal breccia system with gold, high silver, and zinc/lead plus copper indications. The company plans follow-up drilling to test the fault corridor southward, but no near-term production impact is given. | High-grade nickel pig iron prices (SMM 8–12% NPI and Indonesian FOB NPI) fell week-on-week and are expected to stay weak. Elevated Philippine nickel ore CIF costs and Indonesia smelter maintenance/retooling reduce output and worsen smelter losses. Demand is steady but inventories don’t destock strongly; macro support offers only limited lift. | SMM’s weekly review finds Indonesian nickel ore and NPI prices weakening: nickel ore delivered prices eased and NPI averages fell, while rain disrupts logistics and smelters struggle with high ore costs (cost inversion), prompting maintenance/production shifts and reduced procurement. Despite some potential quota approvals, short-term nickel prices face downward pressure.

South32 said it will record a $130 million impairment charge tied to selling its Cerro Matoso ferronickel operations in Colombia to CoreX. The move reflects a sustained nickel market downturn, with producers cutting spending as Indonesia boosts output and prices collapse. South32 also reports declining planned nickel grades. | NexMetals Mining said drilling at the Selebi North deposit in Botswana hit “rich ore,” including multiple intervals with copper grades up to 2.87% and nickel grades up to 1.85%, while extending the orebody down-dip by 315 meters beyond the modeled boundary. The update is generally supportive for copper and nickel sentiment. | NexMetals reported rich results from initial drill holes at its Selebi North copper-nickel deposit in Botswana, including 13.5 meters with 1.13% copper and 1.25% nickel and additional intercepts extending ore down-dip and beyond the modeled resource boundary. Improved resource potential supports bullish sentiment for copper and nickel. | A businessman in Singapore is on trial over an alleged S$1.5 billion nickel investment fraud, with claims that purported physical nickel trading was fictional and investor money was diverted to personal spending. The defence closed its case after the accused declined to testify, while the verdict is pending later. | A Singapore court trial ended for Ng Yu Zhi, accused of masterminding a $1.46b nickel-trading scam. Prosecutors said purported physical nickel trades never occurred and early investors were paid with funds from others.
